data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and ‚Äî when a task calls for it ‚Äî work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy ‚Äî so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown ‚Äî short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` ‚Äî a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions ‚Äî The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style ‚Äî environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox ‚Äî Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox ‚Äî list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting ‚Äî How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session ‚Äî block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check ‚Äî Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors ‚Äî use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag ‚Äî Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) ‚Äî declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects ‚Äî via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch ‚Äî Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git ‚Äî How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents ‚Äî How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work ‚Äî spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope ‚Äî you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0003/sessions/0007-horizon-T2

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint ‚Äî Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end ‚Äî keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug ‚Äî Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run ‚Äî missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors ‚Äî applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor ‚Äî Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier ‚Äî keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber ‚Äî Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever ‚Äî Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] ‚Äî Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task ‚Äî starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper ‚Äî then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] ‚Äî Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] ‚Äî Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits ‚Äî use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` ‚Äî it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
[Request interrupted by user]

# Recommended focus
Task: T2
Title: Prove the FBC lemma ‚Äî flat base change (Stacks 02KH)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange*,AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean

Close the **flat base change** route (Stacks **02KH**, ƒåech separated case) ‚Äî the current PRIMARY directive in the project's `TO_USER.md`.

Prove, for flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$

Remaining open leaves:
- `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks **02KG**, termwise affine base change) via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` in `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange`.
- `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact).

to finish `IsIso bareBC` in `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`. The affine-base separated case suffices for **Kleiman 4.8 Step 1**.

**Done when:** `lake build` green, no new axioms; roadmap item `AJC.fbc` advances/closes.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) ƒåech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **ƒåech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), ƒåech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman ¬ß5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley‚ÄìRosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (K√§hler, rigidity, Abel‚ÄìJacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** K√§hler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel‚ÄìJacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- ‚Ä¶ -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ‚â´ ùüô = A` / `ùüô ‚â´ A = A` when the `ùüô`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(ùü≠).obj M` vs `M`, or a `‚ãô`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom‚â´inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ‚âà 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ‚âà 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p‚ÇÅ).map A_head ‚â´ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p‚ÇÅ).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p‚ÇÅ).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x‚â´y)‚â´z` vs `x‚â´(y‚â´z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `‚ãô`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ‚ãô pullback x ‚âÖ pullback (x‚â´y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso ‚Üí AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) ‚Üí the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ‚äÜ (its labels ‚à™ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) ‚Äî kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ‚âà 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÇ Œ± |>.app c` factors as `adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app _ ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app _) ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (H.map (adj‚ÇÅ.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `ùüô`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ‚ä£ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage ‚Üí unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful ‚Äî Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence ‚Äî components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ‚äì w‚Åª¬πR) = R ‚äì w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [‚Üê Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) ‚Äî 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic ‚Üí CechSectionIdentificationBase ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) ‚Äî olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** ‚Äî do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` ¬ß3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #Œπ` for maximal `M ‚äÜ MvPolynomial Œπ k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `Œπ` ‚Äî the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite Œπ`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ‚â§ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ‚â§ #œÉ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker` (note `P.ker` unfolds to `RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)` by rfl-level abbrevs); quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ‚Ñï‚àû with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` ‚Äî a Noetherian local ring with `finrank Cotangent ‚â§ ringKrullDim` is regular.
- kÃÑ-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ‚ß∏ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) ‚Äî installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ‚ß∏ m)` synthesis inside later applications (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does ‚Äî same idiom as Mathlib's `finite_of_algHom_finiteType_of_isJacobsonRing`).

**Remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`: Stacks 00OF** (localisation of regular local is regular) ‚Äî needs Serre's finite-global-dimension characterisation, absent from Mathlib v4.31 (`IsRegularLocalRing` has only `RegularLocalRing/Defs.lean`). At non-closed points (ii.A) correctly fails (`finrank Cotangent = ht z ‚âÝ n`). Do not attempt 00OF as a side quest ‚Äî it is a large homological build.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' ‚Äî that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session ‚Äî run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action ‚Äî never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

text: I'll start by orienting: reading the project's TO_USER.md, the FBC files, and checking current build state.

input.command: ls && cat TO_USER.md 2>/dev/null | head -60
input.description: List project root and read TO_USER.md

<!-- Shared notice board. Keep to <=2-3 short bullets; delete bullets no longer true. -->

198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî additive functors and the alternating coface complex
199	
200	The relative ƒåech complex `CechComplex` is, by construction
201	(`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`), the alternating coface-map cochain complex of a
202	cosimplicial object. The leaf-2 base change `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` must move the
203	degreewise pullback `g^*` *inside* this `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` construction. The
204	following two general declarations package exactly that move at the right (cosimplicial)
205	altitude, with no reference to schemes: an additive functor `F` commutes with the
206	alternating coface map complex, naturally in the cosimplicial variable. This is
207	Mathlib-absent and is the cosimplicial-natural-iso brick flagged in
208	`analogies/02kh-leaves-304.md` (step (b)). -/

216	/-- The degree-`n` differential of the alternating coface complex is the alternating sum
217	`objD`. Project-local unfolding lemma. -/
218	private theorem alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d
219	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (n : ‚Ñï) :
220	    ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y).d n (n + 1)
221	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y n := by
222	  simp only [alternatingCofaceMapComplex, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj, CochainComplex.of_d]
223	
224	/-- **An additive functor commutes with the alternating coface differential.** For an
225	additive functor `F : C ‚•§ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y`, applying `F` to the
226	degree-`n` alternating coface differential `objD Y n = ‚àë·µ¢ (-1)‚Å± ‚Ä¢ YŒ¥·µ¢` equals the
227	alternating coface differential of the post-composed cosimplicial object `Y ‚ãô F`. This is
228	`F.map_sum` together with `Functor.map_zsmul` (both available since `F` is additive).
229	Project-local. -/
230	theorem map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD (F : C ‚•§ D) [F.Additive]
231	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (i : ‚Ñï) :
232	    F.map (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y i)
233	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
234	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y) i := by
235	  rw [AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Functor.map_sum]
236	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
237	  intro k _
238	  rw [Functor.map_zsmul]
239	  rfl
240	
241	/-- **Additive functors commute with `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.** For an additive
242	functor `F : C ‚•§ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y` in `C`, applying `F` degreewise to the
243	alternating coface map cochain complex of `Y` yields the alternating coface map cochain
244	complex of the post-composed cosimplicial object `F ‚àò Y`:
245	`F.mapHomologicalComplex (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Y) ‚âÖ alternatingCofaceMapComplex (F ‚àò Y)`.
246	The degreewise components are identities (the degree-`n` terms are `F.obj (Y.obj [n])` on
247	both sides) and the differential compatibility is `map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD`.
248	This is the cosimplicial-altitude brick (step (b)) used to push `g^*` into the relative
249	ƒåech complex `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. Project-local Mathlib supplement. -/

1454	Given the cartesian square

1459	  S' --g---> S

1463	unconditional ƒåech higher direct images is an isomorphism

850	/-- **Pullback telescope across the restricted cartesian square** (the pseudofunctor leg of the
851	open-immersion Beck‚ÄìChevalley).  Using only the pseudofunctor structure of `pullback`
852	(`pullbackComp`, `pullbackCongr`) and the square equation `p' ‚â´ g' = gV ‚â´ p`, the iterated
853	pullback `p'^*(g'^* F)` is canonically isomorphic to `gV^*(p^* F)`:
854	```
855	  p'^*(g'^* F) ‚âÖ (p' ‚â´ g')^* F = (gV ‚â´ p)^* F ‚âÖ gV^*(p^* F).
856	```
857	Sorry-free, build-cheap (no flatness, no affineness).  Together with `openImmersion_bareBC` this
858	collapses `openImmersion_beckChevalley` to the single obligation `IsIso (openImmersion_bareBC ‚Ä¶)`.

883	/-! ## Stage-2 reduction: the bare mate factors as `unit ‚â´ iso`
884	
885	The mate formula (`mateEquiv_apply`) exhibits `openImmersion_bareBC` at each object `c` as
886	```

890	`pullback p ‚ä£ pushforward p` is an isomorphism ‚Äî it is conjugate, under the
891	`leftAdjointUniq` comparison `restrictFunctorIsoPullback p`, to the counit of the
892	site-level `restrictAdjunction p`, invertible in Mathlib.  Hence the *only* non-iso
893	factor is the leading `p'`-unit, and `IsIso (bareBC.app c)` collapses to the single
894	node `IsIso (unit_{p'}.app (g'^*(p_* c)))` ‚Äî "`g'^*(p_* c)` is in the essential image
895	of `p'_*`".  This is a strict sharpening of the blueprint Stage-2 chain
896	(`lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso`): the legs-are-restrictions reduction
897	(`lem:openimm_bareBC_legs_restriction`) is here replaced by the exact mate
898	factorization, so the member/assembly work only ever has to handle the unit. -/

1081	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî refining affine cover for the open-immersion
1082	Beck‚ÄìChevalley assembly (Stage-2 reduction of `openImmersion_beckChevalley`)
1083	
1084	The `IsIso bareBC` residual of `openImmersion_beckChevalley` is checked *cover-locally* on `X'`
1085	(keystone `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_restrict`).  This section supplies the geometric
1086	input for that assembly: an affine open cover `{W‚±º}` of `X'` such that each `g'|_{W‚±º}` lands in an
1087	affine open `U‚±º ‚äÜ X` (so `g'|_{W‚±º}` is a map of affine schemes, a `Spec`-map).  It is pure geometry
1088	(no flatness, no quasi-coherence), obtained by pulling the standard affine cover of `X` back along
1089	`g'` and refining each preimage to affine members. -/
1090	
1091	/-- **Packaged output of `openImmersion_refiningAffineCover`.**  An affine open cover of `X'`
1092	together with, for each member `W‚±º`, an affine open `U‚±º ‚äÜ X` containing the image `g'(W‚±º)` (recorded
1093	as the range containment `Set.range (W‚±º.f ‚â´ g').base ‚äÜ U‚±º`).  Project-local packaging of the
1094	Stage-2 geometric datum of `openImmersion_beckChevalley`; blueprint
1095	`lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover`. -/
1096	structure OpenImmersionRefiningAffineCover (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) where
1097	  /-- the affine open cover of `X'`. -/
1098	  cover : X'.OpenCover
1099	  /-- every member of `cover` is affine. -/
1100	  isAffine_cover : ‚àÄ j, IsAffine (cover.X j)
1101	  /-- the containing affine open of `X` for each member. -/
1102	  U : cover.I‚ÇÄ ‚Üí X.Opens
1103	  /-- each `U j` is an affine open. -/
1104	  isAffineOpen_U : ‚àÄ j, IsAffineOpen (U j)
1105	  /-- the image `g'(W‚±º)` is contained in `U j`. -/
1106	  le_U : ‚àÄ j, Set.range (cover.f j ‚â´ g').base ‚äÜ (U j : Set X)
1107	
1108	/-- The pullback of the standard affine cover of `X` along `g'`, refined to affine members. -/
1109	private noncomputable abbrev pullbackAffineRefinementCover (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) : X'.OpenCover :=
1110	  (Scheme.OpenCover.affineRefinement (X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullback‚ÇÅ g')).openCover
1111	
1112	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1113	/-- **Affine cover of `X'` refining preimages of affine opens of `X`** (blueprint
1114	`lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover`).  For any `g' : X' ‚ü∂ X` there is an affine open cover `{W‚±º}` of
1115	`X'` together with, for each `j`, an affine open `U‚±º ‚äÜ X` with `g'(W‚±º) ‚äÜ U‚±º`; hence `g'|_{W‚±º}` is a
1116	map of affine schemes.  Constructed by pulling `X.affineOpenCover` back along `g'`
1117	(`OpenCover.pullback‚ÇÅ`) ‚Äî whose member over index `i` maps, via `Cover.pullbackHom`, into the affine
1118	`X.affineOpenCover.X i` ‚Äî and refining each (possibly non-affine) preimage member to affine pieces
1119	(`OpenCover.affineRefinement`).  The containment is the range monotonicity
1120	`Set.range (œÜ ‚â´ g'(=‚Ä¶‚â´ U·µ¢.Œπ)).base ‚äÜ Set.range U·µ¢.Œπ.base = U·µ¢`.  Pure geometry ‚Äî no flatness or
1121	quasi-coherence.  Project-local: the Stage-2 geometric input of `openImmersion_beckChevalley`. -/
1122	noncomputable def openImmersion_refiningAffineCover (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) :
1123	    OpenImmersionRefiningAffineCover g' where
1124	  cover := pullbackAffineRefinementCover g'
1125	  isAffine_cover j := by infer_instance
1126	  U j := (X.affineOpenCover.openCover.f j.1).opensRange
1127	  isAffineOpen_U j := isAffineOpen_opensRange _
1128	  le_U j := by
1129	    have hcomp : (pullbackAffineRefinementCover g').f j ‚â´ g'
1130	        = (((X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullback‚ÇÅ g').X j.1).affineCover.f j.2
1131	            ‚â´ X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullbackHom g' j.1)
1132	          ‚â´ X.affineOpenCover.openCover.f j.1 := by
1133	      have hf : (pullbackAffineRefinementCover g').f j
1134	          = ((X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullback‚ÇÅ g').X j.1).affineCover.f j.2
1135	          ‚â´ (X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullback‚ÇÅ g').f j.1 := rfl
1136	      rw [hf, Category.assoc, ‚Üê Scheme.Cover.pullbackHom_map, ‚Üê Category.assoc]
1137	    rintro x ‚ü®y, rfl‚ü©
1138	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange]
1139	    exact ‚ü®(((X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullback‚ÇÅ g').X j.1).affineCover.f j.2
1140	            ‚â´ X.affineOpenCover.openCover.pullbackHom g' j.1).base y,
1141	      by rw [hcomp, Scheme.Hom.comp_base]; rfl‚ü©
1142	
1143	/-- **Open-immersion Beck‚ÄìChevalley over a restricted cartesian square** (Stacks 02KG; carved
1144	block `lem:openimm_beckchevalley`).  Let `p : V ‚ü∂ X` be an *open immersion* and let the square
1145	```
1146	  V' --gV--> V
1147	  |p'        |p
1148	  v          v
1149	  X' --g'--> X
1150	```
1151	be cartesian (`hsq`), so `p'` is the open immersion onto the preimage `(g')‚Åª¬π(V)`.  Then there is
1152	a Beck‚ÄìChevalley isomorphism of `O_{X'}`-modules
1153	```
1154	  (g')^*(p_* p^* F) ‚âÖ p'_* p'^*((g')^* F),
1155	```
1156	i.e. `(pullback g').obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk p)) ‚âÖ pushPullObj ((pullback g').obj F)
1157	(Over.mk p')`.

346	noncomputable def cech_degree_affine_baseChange {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
347	    (œÜ : R ‚ü∂ A) (œà : R ‚ü∂ R') (œÅ : A ‚ü∂ B) (œÉ : R' ‚ü∂ B)
348	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout œÜ œà œÅ œÉ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
349	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œà)).obj
350	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M)) ‚âÖ
351	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÉ)).obj
352	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÅ)).obj (tilde M)) :=

355	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî the abstract‚Üíaffine `pushPullObj ‚âÖ tilde` bridge
356	
357	The two degreewise Beck‚ÄìChevalley leaves (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`,
358	`twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) reduce, on the affine model of the cover, to the sorry-free affine
359	brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`. The missing edge (Stacks 01I8 / 01BG) is the identification
360	of the abstract push‚Äìpull data of a fibre power with the `tilde`-model over `Spec`. We build it at
361	the two well-typed altitudes flagged in `analogies/fbc-pushpull-tilde-317.md`:
362	
363	* **altitude 1** (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`): the restriction `p^* F = (V.Œπ)^* F` of a
364	  quasi-coherent `F` to an affine open `V` of `X`, *pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso*
365	  `V ‚âÖ Spec Œì(X, V)` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`), is `tilde` of its global sections over
366	  `Spec Œì(X, V)`. Quasi-coherence is preserved by pullback along the open immersion `V.Œπ`
367	  (`pullback_isQuasicoherent`/`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`) and by pushforward along the
368	  iso `isoSpec` (`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the affine structure theorem 01I8
369	  (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, unconditional via the live instance
370	  `isIso_fromTildeŒì_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
371	* **altitude 2** (`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde`): over the affine base `S = Spec R`, the
372	  pushed-forward push‚Äìpull object `f_*(p_* p^* F) = f_*((V.Œπ)_* (V.Œπ)^* F)` is `(Spec œÜ)_* (tilde N)`
373	  ‚Äî collapse `f_* ‚àò (V.Œπ)_*` to `(V.Œπ ‚â´ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`, factor
374	  `V.Œπ ‚â´ f = isoSpec.hom ‚â´ Spec.map œÜ` (with `œÜ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ‚â´ f)`,
375	  `Spec.map_preimage`), split off `(Spec.map œÜ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again, and feed altitude 1
376	  through `(Spec.map œÜ)_*`. This is exactly the form the brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`
377	  consumes. See blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`, `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde`.
378	
379	All ingredients are axiom-clean project infrastructure: `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`
380	(`CechTermAcyclic`), `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (`OpenImmersionPushforward`),
381	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (`QcohTildeSections`, the unconditional 01I8 structure theorem).
382	-/

384	/-- **Pullback preserves quasi-coherence** (Stacks 01BG, open case).  For an open `V` of `X` and a
385	quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the restriction `(V.Œπ)^* F` is quasi-coherent on `V`.  This is the
386	open-immersion case the fibre-power projections of the {\v C}ech nerve require (each
387	`Y_n = U_{i‚ÇÄ} ‚à© ‚ãØ ‚à© U_{i‚Çô} ‚Ü™ X` is an open immersion); the general-morphism case is absent from both
388	Mathlib and the project (the abstract left-adjoint route via `Presentation.map` only transports a
389	*global* presentation, whereas quasi-coherence is *local*).  A thin re-export of
390	`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` (proved via `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover).
391	Project-local; blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`. -/
392	theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent (V : X.Opens) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
393	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F).IsQuasicoherent :=
394	  isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens V F hF
395	
396	/-- **Altitude 1 of the bridge: `(V.Œπ)^* F` pushed to `Spec Œì(X,V)` is `tilde N`** (Stacks 01I8).
397	For a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules` and an affine open `V` of `X`, the restriction `(V.Œπ)^* F`,
398	pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso `isoSpec : V ‚âÖ Spec Œì(X, V)`, is canonically isomorphic to
399	the `tilde` of its module of global sections `N = Œì(Spec Œì(X,V), -)`.  The pullback is quasi-coherent
400	(`pullback_isQuasicoherent`) and quasi-coherence is preserved by the iso-pushforward
401	(`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the unconditional affine structure theorem 01I8
402	(`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, via the live instance `isIso_fromTildeŒì_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
403	Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 1). -/
404	noncomputable def pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
405	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
406	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F) ‚âÖ
407	      tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj
408	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F))) :=
409	  haveI : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj
410	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F)).IsQuasicoherent :=
411	    pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh hV.isoSpec ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F)
412	      (pullback_isQuasicoherent V F hF)
413	  qcoh_iso_tilde_sections _

415	/-- **Altitude 2 of the bridge: `f_*(p_* p^* F) ‚âÖ (Spec œÜ)_* (tilde N)`** (Stacks 01I8, assembled
416	pushed-forward level).  Over the affine base `S = Spec R`, with `V` an affine open of `X` and
417	`œÜ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ‚â´ f) : R ‚ü∂ Œì(X, V)` the ring map presenting the composite
418	`isoSpec.inv ‚â´ V.Œπ ‚â´ f = fromSpec ‚â´ f` as `Spec.map œÜ`, the pushed-forward push‚Äìpull object
419	`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ))` is canonically isomorphic to
420	`(pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde N)`.
421	
422	The construction: collapse `f_* ‚àò (V.Œπ)_*` to `(V.Œπ ‚â´ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`; rewrite
423	`V.Œπ ‚â´ f = isoSpec.hom ‚â´ Spec.map œÜ` (from `Spec.map_preimage` and `isoSpec_inv_Œπ`) by
424	`pushforwardCongr`; split off `(Spec.map œÜ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again (leaving the altitude-1
425	domain `(isoSpec.hom)_* ((V.Œπ)^* F)`); then push altitude 1 (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`) through
426	`(Spec.map œÜ)_*`.  The right-hand side is exactly the form consumed by the brick
427	`cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 2). -/
428	noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
429	    (f : X ‚ü∂ Spec R) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
430	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
431	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ)) ‚âÖ
432	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ f)))).obj
433	        (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj
434	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F)))) :=
435	  have heq : V.Œπ ‚â´ f = hV.isoSpec.hom ‚â´ Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ f)) := by
436	    rw [Spec.map_preimage, ‚Üê IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_Œπ hV, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
437	  (pushforwardComp V.Œπ f).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F) ‚â™‚â´
438	    (pushforwardCongr heq).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F) ‚â™‚â´
439	    (pushforwardComp hV.isoSpec.hom (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ f)))).symm.app
440	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F) ‚â™‚â´
441	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ f)))).mapIso
442	      (pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde F hF hV)

444	/-- **Altitude 2 over an abstract affine base** (Stacks 01I8, abstract-`S` generalization of
445	`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde`).  For a *separated* `f : X ‚ü∂ S` with `S` an **abstract** affine
446	scheme (`[IsAffine S]`, so `S` need not be a literal `Spec`), write `e_S := S.isoSpec : S ‚âÖ Spec Œì(S)`
447	for the canonical affine identification.  The pushed-forward push‚Äìpull object
448	`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ))` is canonically isomorphic to the altitude-2
449	`(Spec œÜ)_*(tilde N)` form, **transported back along `e_S‚Åª¬π`** so it lands in `O_S`-modules rather
450	than `O_{Spec Œì(S)}`-modules, where `œÜ := Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ (f ‚â´ e_S.hom))` presents
451	`(e_S ‚àò f) ‚àò (isoSpec ‚àò j_V)` as `Spec œÜ`.

453	Construction (‚â§10 lines): the composite `f ‚â´ e_S.hom : X ‚ü∂ Spec Œì(S)` has a *literal* affine base,
454	so the literal-Spec bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` applies to it; then conjugate by
455	`(pushforward e_S.inv)` and collapse `pushforward e_S.hom ‚ãô pushforward e_S.inv ‚âÖ id` via
456	`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`/`pushforwardId` (the `e_S` cancellation).  This is the form the
457	heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` consumes (the abstract-`S` ‚Üî `Spec R` transport, applied
458	once for both `f` and `f'`).  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde_affine`. -/
459	noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine [IsAffine S]
460	    (f : X ‚ü∂ S) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
461	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
462	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ)) ‚âÖ
463	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).obj
464	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage
465	            (hV.fromSpec ‚â´ (f ‚â´ S.isoSpec.hom))))).obj
466	          (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj
467	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj
468	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F))))) :=
469	  -- `collapse : (e_S‚Åª¬π)_* ((f ‚â´ e_S)_* P) ‚âÖ f_* P`, the `e_S` cancellation; take `.symm` to start
470	  -- from `f_* P`, then push the literal bridge through `(e_S‚Åª¬π)_*`.
471	  ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).mapIso
472	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f S.isoSpec.hom).symm.app
473	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ))) ‚â™‚â´
474	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp S.isoSpec.hom S.isoSpec.inv).app
475	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ))) ‚â™‚â´
476	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id).app
477	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ))) ‚â™‚â´
478	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId S).app
479	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.Œπ)))).symm ‚â™‚â´
480	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).mapIso
481	      (pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde (f ‚â´ S.isoSpec.hom) F hF hV)

483	/-- **ƒåech intersection opens are affine** (separated case).  For a separated `f : X ‚ü∂ S`
484	with `S` affine and an affine open cover `ùí∞` of `X`, every finite nonempty fibre-power
485	intersection open `coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ = ‚®Ö k, (ùí∞.f (œÉ k)).opensRange` is affine.  `X` is
486	separated over the terminal scheme (`f` separated and `S` affine ‚Äî hence separated ‚Äî so the
487	composite `terminal.from X = f ‚â´ terminal.from S` is separated), so the absolute diagonal of
488	`X` is a closed immersion, hence affine; finite intersections of affine opens of a scheme with
489	affine diagonal are affine (`IsAffineOpen.iInf`), and each member open is affine as the range of
490	an open immersion out of the affine `ùí∞.X (œÉ k)` (`isAffineOpen_opensRange`).  This is the
491	affineness ingredient consumed by the affine-reduction heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`.
492	Project-local; blueprint `lem:cech_degree_affine_baseChange` (affineness side-condition). -/
493	theorem coverInterOpen_isAffine (f : X ‚ü∂ S) [IsSeparated f] [IsAffine S]
494	    (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [‚àÄ i, IsAffine (ùí∞.X i)] {Œ∫ : Type} [Finite Œ∫] [Nonempty Œ∫]
495	    (œÉ : Œ∫ ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ) : IsAffineOpen (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ) := by
496	  -- `X` is separated over the terminal scheme: `terminal.from X = f ‚â´ terminal.from S`, with
497	  -- `f` separated and `terminal.from S` separated (`S` affine ‚üπ `S.IsSeparated`).
498	  haveI hsep : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := by
499	    rw [‚Üê terminal.comp_from f]
500	    exact IsSeparated.comp_iff.mpr ‚ÄπIsSeparated f‚Ä∫
501	  -- hence the absolute diagonal is a closed immersion (‚üπ affine), unlocking `IsAffineOpen.iInf`.
502	  haveI : IsClosedImmersion (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X)) :=
503	    IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal
504	  exact IsAffineOpen.iInf (fun k => isAffineOpen_opensRange (ùí∞.f (œÉ k)))
505	
506	/-- **Restriction of the cartesian square over an affine intersection open is a (ring) pushout**
507	(Stacks 02KG; carved block `lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout`).  Restricting the global
508	cartesian square `X' = X √ó_S S' ‚Üí X` over the ƒåech fibre-power intersection open
509	`V = coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ ‚Ü™ X` (open immersion `j_œÉ`) replaces `X` by `V` and `X'` by the fibre
510	product `X' √ó_X V`, and the restricted square
511	```
512	  X' √ó_X V --pullback.fst--> V
513	   |pullback.snd             |j_œÉ
514	   v                         v
515	  X'  --------g'------------> X
516	```
517	is cartesian.  This is the geometric half of the carved block: under `[IsSeparated f]`,
518	`[IsAffine S]`, `[IsAffine S']` and an affine cover, `V` is affine (`coverInterOpen_isAffine`)
519	and `X' √ó_X V` is affine, so applying global sections turns this cartesian square of affines into
520	the cocartesian (pushout) square of rings `R ‚Üí A_œÉ`, `R ‚Üí R'`, `A_œÉ ‚Üí A_œÉ ‚äó_R R'` via the
521	affine-pullback ‚Üî ring-pushout equivalence `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`
522	(`lem:commRingCat_isPushout_iff_mathlib`, `\mathlibok`) ‚Äî exactly the affine pushout square
523	consumed by `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Sorry-free: the restricted square is a pullback by
524	construction (`IsPullback.of_hasPullback`).  Project-local; blueprint
525	`lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout`. -/
526	theorem restrictedCartesianAffinePushout (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X)
527	    (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) {Œ∫ : Type} (œÉ : Œ∫ ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ) :
528	    IsPullback (pullback.snd g' (Scheme.Opens.Œπ (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)))
529	      (pullback.fst g' (Scheme.Opens.Œπ (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)))
530	      (Scheme.Opens.Œπ (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)) g' :=
531	  (IsPullback.of_hasPullback g' (Scheme.Opens.Œπ (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ))).flip

data.content:
set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Restriction of an `ùí™‚Çì`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ‚•§ X.Modules :=
  letI Œ± : X.presheaf ‚ü∂ f.opensFunctor.op ‚ãô Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ‚ü®Functor.whiskerRight Œ± (forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)‚ü©

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

/-- The sections of the restriction of `M` over `U` are isomorphic to `Œì(M, f ''·µÅ U). -/
def restrictAppIso (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) : Œì(M.restrict f, U) ‚âÖ Œì(M, f ''·µÅ U) :=
  Iso.refl _

@[elementwise (attr := simp), reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma smul_restrictAppIso_hom (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) (r : Œì(X, U)) :
    dsimp% (M.restrict f).smul r ‚â´ (M.restrictAppIso f U).hom =
      (M.restrictAppIso f U).hom ‚â´ M.smul ((f.appIso U).inv r) :=
  rfl

@[elementwise (attr := simp), reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma smul_restrictAppIso_inv (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) (r : Œì(Y, f ''·µÅ U)) :
    M.smul r ‚â´ (M.restrictAppIso f U).inv =
      (M.restrictAppIso f U).inv ‚â´ (M.restrict f).smul ((f.appIso U).hom r) := by
  simp [‚Üê cancel_mono (M.restrictAppIso f U).hom]

@[elementwise (attr := simp), reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma map_restrictAppIso_hom (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens}
    (hUV : Opposite.op V ‚ü∂ .op U) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map hUV ‚â´ (M.restrictAppIso f U).hom =
      (M.restrictAppIso f V).hom ‚â´
      M.presheaf.map (.op <| homOfLE <| Scheme.Hom.image_mono _ (leOfHom hUV.unop)) := by
  rfl

@[elementwise (attr := simp), reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma restrictAppIso_inv_map (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (hUV : .op V ‚ü∂ .op U) :
    (M.restrictAppIso f V).inv ‚â´ (M.restrict f).presheaf.map hUV =
      M.presheaf.map (.op <| homOfLE <| Scheme.Hom.image_mono _ (leOfHom hUV.unop)) ‚â´
      (M.restrictAppIso f U).inv :=
  rfl

/-- Avoid using this. Use the isomorphism `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictAppIso`
instead. -/
lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Œì(M.restrict f, U) = Œì(M, f ''·µÅ U) := rfl

/-- Avoid using this. Use the isomorphism `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictAppIso`
instead. -/
lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ‚ü∂ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ‚ãô restrictFunctor f ‚âÖ ùü≠ _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ‚â™‚â´ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ‚Ü¶ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ‚Ü¶ rfl) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ‚â™‚â´ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ‚ä£ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  ¬∑ ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  ¬∑ ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ‚â´ f.app _ ‚â´
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = ùüô _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, ‚Üê X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ‚âÖ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (ùüô X) ‚âÖ ùü≠ _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ‚Ü¶ eqToIso (by simp))) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [‚Üê Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = ùüô X ''·µÅ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show ùüô X ''·µÅ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ‚â´ g) ‚âÖ restrictFunctor g ‚ãô restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ‚ãô g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ‚Ü¶ eqToIso (by simp)) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [‚Üê Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ‚â™‚â´
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ‚âÖ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ‚Ü¶ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

theorem range_pullbackSnd :
    Set.range (pullback.snd f g) = g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ f.opensRange := by
  rw [‚Üê show _ = (pullback.snd f g).base from
    PreservesPullback.iso_hom_snd Scheme.forgetToTop f g, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp,
    Set.range_eq_univ.mpr, ‚Üê @Set.preimage_univ _ _ (pullback.fst f.base g.base)]
  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11224): was `rw`
  ¬∑ erw [TopCat.pullback_snd_image_fst_preimage]
    rw [Set.image_univ]
    rfl
  rw [‚Üê TopCat.epi_iff_surjective]
  infer_instance

theorem _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.opensRange_pullbackSnd :
    (pullback.snd f g).opensRange = g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ f.opensRange :=
  Opens.ext (range_pullbackSnd f g)

variable (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) (g : Y ‚ü∂ Z) (h : Z ‚ü∂ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ‚•§ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Œì((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Œì(M, f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ‚ü∂ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (œÜ : M ‚ü∂ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map œÜ).app U = œÜ.app (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ‚•§ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ‚ä£ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (ùüô X) ‚âÖ ùü≠ _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = ùüô _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = ùüô _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (ùüô X) ‚âÖ ùü≠ _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (ùüô X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ‚ãô pushforward g ‚âÖ pushforward (f ‚â´ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = ùüô _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = ùüô _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ‚ãô pullback f ‚âÖ pullback (f ‚â´ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ‚âÖ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ‚ñ∏ rfl)) ‚â™‚â´
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ‚ñ∏ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ‚ñ∏ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ‚âÖ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ‚ñ∏ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ‚â´ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
    (pullbackComp f (g ‚â´ h)).inv ‚â´

data.content:
assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it
to an *isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre
vanishing for `U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.

The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y`. -/

/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y`, the
pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward œÜ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
along `œÜ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    X.Modules ‚âå Y.Modules :=
  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward œÜ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward œÜ.inv)
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ‚â™‚â´ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr œÜ.hom_inv_id.symm ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp œÜ.hom œÜ.inv).symm)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp œÜ.inv œÜ.hom ‚â™‚â´ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr œÜ.inv_hom_id ‚â™‚â´
      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)

/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward œÜ.hom`. -/
noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor.Additive :=
  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward œÜ.hom).Additive

/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
`Ext A B n ‚âÉ+ Ext (œÜ_* A) (œÜ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y)
    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ‚âÉ+
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor.obj A)
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor.obj B) n :=
  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor A B n)

/-- **Need #1 ‚Äî `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
`U`, the canonical iso `U ‚âÖ Spec Œì(U,ùí™)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
`Ext^q_U(A, B) ‚âÖ Ext^q_{Spec Œì U}(œÜ_* A, œÜ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
iff its transport over `Spec Œì U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.

The remaining identification of the transported arguments ‚Äî `œÜ_*(jShriekOU V) ‚âÖ jShriekOU V'` for
`V'` the (affine) image open, and quasi-coherence of `œÜ_* H` ‚Äî is handed off (see the task-result
file); it is orthogonal to this transport and is the geometric naturality of `j_!` under a scheme
iso. -/

theorem coversTop_preimage {Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ‚ü∂ X)
    (M : X.Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (q.X i)) := by
  intro W' y hy
  obtain ‚ü®U_X, _fX, hsieve, hgyU‚ü© := q.coversTop ‚ä§ (g.base y) (by trivial)
  rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hsieve
  obtain ‚ü®i, ‚ü®hUi‚ü©‚ü© := hsieve
  refine ‚ü®W' ‚äì (g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (q.X i)), homOfLE inf_le_left, ?_, hy, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff]
    exact ‚ü®i, ‚ü®homOfLE inf_le_right‚ü©‚ü©
  ¬∑ change g.base y ‚àà q.X i
    exact leOfHom hUi hgyU

set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
/-- **(Piece A, L6) Pullback of a quasi-coherent sheaf along an open immersion is quasi-coherent.**
For an open immersion `g : Y ‚ü∂ X` and `M` quasi-coherent on `X`, the pullback `(pullback g).obj M` is
quasi-coherent. Choose quasi-coherence data `q` for `M` (index shrunk to the site universe); the
preimage family `{g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (q.X i)}` covers `Y` (`coversTop_preimage`) and on each member the slice is
quasi-coherent (`isQuasicoherent_over_preimage`), so `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` applies.
Project-local: Mathlib has no QC-pullback lemma. -/
theorem isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion {Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ‚ü∂ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent] :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ‚ü®q‚ü© : Nonempty M.QuasicoherentData :=
    SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData
  set qs := q.shrink with hqs
  haveI : ‚àÄ i, (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M).over (g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (qs.X i))).IsQuasicoherent :=
    fun i => isQuasicoherent_over_preimage g M qs i
  exact SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj M)
    (fun i => g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (qs.X i)) (coversTop_preimage g M qs)

/-- **(Piece A, target) Quasi-coherence is preserved under pullback along `fromSpec`.**
For `M` quasi-coherent on `X` and an affine open `U`, the pullback of `M` along the affine immersion
`hU.fromSpec : Spec Œì(X, U) ‚ü∂ X` is quasi-coherent. The `g := hU.fromSpec` instance of
`isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion` (`fromSpec` is an open immersion). This is the
QC-pullback input the gap2 close feeds to gap1. Project-local. -/
theorem isQuasicoherent_pullback_fromSpec (M : X.Modules) [M.IsQuasicoherent]
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :

-- Heartbeat bumps: the per-slice presentation transport triggers synthesis of the sliced/doubly
-- sliced `HasSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective`/`HasSheafCompose` instances over `(gt Y).over V·µ¢`,
-- whose default-heartbeat search times out (it succeeds when bumped).
/-- **Pushforward along a scheme iso preserves quasi-coherence** (blueprint
`lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`; Need #1 `hqc`).  Quasi-coherence is local: move each local
presentation of `H` (over the cover member `U·µ¢`) across the colimit-preserving pullback
`pullback œà_r`, then across the comparison iso `pushforwardSlicePullbackIso` to a presentation of
`(Œ¶ H).over V·µ¢`; conclude per-slice quasi-coherence and glue via
`pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh`. -/
lemma pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (œÜ : X ‚âÖ Y) (H : X.Modules)
    (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso œÜ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ‚ü®qcd‚ü© := hH.nonempty_quasicoherentData
  refine pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh œÜ H qcd (fun i => ?_)
  -- Per-slice: build a presentation of `(Œ¶ H).over (œÜ.inv‚Åª¬π·µÅ qcd.X i)`.
  haveI hFinal : (sliceOversEquiv œÜ (qcd.X i)).functor.Final := inferInstance
  -- the unit comparison `Œ∑ : (pullback œà_r).obj (unit) ‚âÖ unit` (iso since the slice functor is Final).
  have Œ∑ : (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom œÜ (qcd.X i))).obj
        (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over (qcd.X i))) ‚âÖ
      SheafOfModules.unit (Y.ringCatSheaf.over (œÜ.inv ‚Åª¬π·µÅ qcd.X i)) :=
    asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom œÜ (qcd.X i)))
  -- transport the local presentation of `H.over U·µ¢` across the colimit-preserving pullback...
  have P1 : ((SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom œÜ (qcd.X i))).obj
      (H.over (qcd.X i))).Presentation :=
    SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} (qcd.presentation i) (SheafOfModules.pullback.{u} (sliceStructureSheafHom œÜ (qcd.X i))) Œ∑.symm

    IsPullback (Spec.map (pushout.inl f g))
      (Spec.map (pushout.inr f g)) (Spec.map f) (Spec.map g) := by
  apply isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout
  exact IsPushout.of_hasPushout f g

theorem diagonal_isAffine_iff_forall_isAffineOpen_inf [IsAffine Y] (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) :
    AffineTargetMorphismProperty.diagonal (fun X _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ IsAffine X) f ‚Üî
      ‚àÄ (U V : X.Opens), IsAffineOpen U ‚Üí IsAffineOpen V ‚Üí IsAffineOpen (U ‚äì V) := by
  delta AffineTargetMorphismProperty.diagonal
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro H U V hU hV
    dsimp at H
    haveI : IsAffine _ := hU
    haveI : IsAffine _ := hV
    let g : pullback U.Œπ V.Œπ ‚ü∂ X := pullback.fst _ _ ‚â´ U.Œπ
    have := IsOpenImmersion.isPullback (X.homOfLE inf_le_left) (X.homOfLE inf_le_right)
      U.Œπ V.Œπ (by simp) (by ext; simp)
    exact .of_isIso this.isoPullback.hom
  ¬∑ introv H h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ
    have : IsAffineOpen (pullback.fst f‚ÇÅ f‚ÇÇ ‚â´ f‚ÇÅ).opensRange := by
      convert! H _ _ (isAffineOpen_opensRange f‚ÇÅ) (isAffineOpen_opensRange f‚ÇÇ) using 1
      exact Opens.ext (IsOpenImmersion.range_pullback_to_base_of_left _ _)
    change IsAffine _ at this
    exact .of_isIso (pullback.fst f‚ÇÅ f‚ÇÇ ‚â´ f‚ÇÅ).isoOpensRange.hom

theorem isAffineHom_diagonal_iff {f : X ‚ü∂ Y} :
    IsAffineHom (pullback.diagonal f) ‚Üî
      ‚àÄ (U : Y.Opens), IsAffineOpen U ‚Üí ‚àÄ V‚ÇÅ ‚â§ f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U, ‚àÄ V‚ÇÇ ‚â§ f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U,

of affine opens of `X` are also affine. -/

@[simps!]
def forgetToTop : Scheme ‚•§ TopCat :=
  Scheme.forgetToLocallyRingedSpace ‚ãô LocallyRingedSpace.forgetToTop

/-- An isomorphism of schemes induces a homeomorphism of the underlying topological spaces. -/
noncomputable def homeoOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) : X ‚âÉ‚Çú Y :=
  TopCat.homeoOfIso (forgetToTop.mapIso e)

@[simp]
lemma coe_homeoOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    ‚áë(homeoOfIso e) = e.hom := rfl

@[simp]
lemma coe_homeoOfIso_symm {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    ‚áë(homeoOfIso e.symm) = e.inv := rfl

@[simp]
lemma homeoOfIso_symm {X Y : Scheme} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    (homeoOfIso e).symm = homeoOfIso e.symm := rfl

lemma homeoOfIso_apply {X Y : Scheme} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) (x : X) :
    homeoOfIso e x = e.hom x := rfl

alias _root_.CategoryTheory.Iso.schemeIsoToHomeo := homeoOfIso

/-- An isomorphism of schemes induces a homeomorphism of the underlying topological spaces. -/
noncomputable def Hom.homeomorph {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [IsIso (C := Scheme) f] :
    X ‚âÉ‚Çú Y :=
  (asIso f).schemeIsoToHomeo

@[simp]
lemma Hom.homeomorph_apply {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [IsIso (C := Scheme) f] (x) :
    f.homeomorph x = f x := rfl

instance hasCoeToTopCat : CoeOut Scheme TopCat where
  coe X := X.carrier

/-- forgetful functor to `TopCat` is the same as coercion -/
unif_hint forgetToTop_obj_eq_coe (X : Scheme) where ‚ä¢ forgetToTop.obj X ‚âü (X : TopCat)

/-- The forgetful functor from `Scheme` to `Type`. -/
variable {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) (g : Y ‚ü∂ Z)

/-- A morphism of schemes is surjective if the underlying map is. -/
@[mk_iff]
class Surjective : Prop where
  surj : Function.Surjective f

lemma surjective_eq_topologically :
    @Surjective = topologically Function.Surjective := by ext; exact surjective_iff _

@[grind .]
lemma Scheme.Hom.surjective (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) [Surjective f] : Function.Surjective f :=
  Surjective.surj

instance (priority := 100) [IsIso f] : Surjective f := ‚ü®f.homeomorph.surjective‚ü©

instance [Surjective f] [Surjective g] : Surjective (f ‚â´ g) := ‚ü®g.surjective.comp f.surjective‚ü©

lemma Surjective.of_comp [Surjective (f ‚â´ g)] : Surjective g where
  surj := Function.Surjective.of_comp (g := f) (f ‚â´ g).surjective

instance (priority := low) [Nonempty X] [Subsingleton Y] (f : X ‚ü∂ Y) :
    Surjective f := ‚ü®Function.surjective_to_subsingleton _‚ü©

lemma Surjective.comp_iff [Surjective f] : Surjective (f ‚â´ g) ‚Üî Surjective g :=
  ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ of_comp f g, fun _ ‚Ü¶ inferInstance‚ü©
