/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.OpenImmersionPushforward
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechTermAcyclic

/-!
# Pullback of a pushforward from an affine open is a pushforward (essential-image form)

This file supplies the affine-local heart of the Stage-2 essential-image reduction of the
open-immersion Beck‚ÄìChevalley comparison (Stacks 02KG/02KH, blueprint
`lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine`): for a morphism `gU : W ‚ü∂ Y` of *affine* schemes, an
affine open `VU ‚äÜ Y` and a quasi-coherent `K` on `VU`, the pulled-back pushforward
`gU^*((VU.Œπ)_* K)` lies in the essential image of the pushforward along the preimage open
`gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU ‚Ü™ W` (`pullback_pushforward_affineOpen_essImage`).  The content is the sorry-free
affine termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` over the *abstract* ring
pushout `Œì(W) ‚äó_{Œì(Y)} Œì(VU)`, whose `Spec` is identified with the preimage open by the
cartesianness of `Spec` of a pushout (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`) and the range
computation of the base-changed open immersion (`IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`).

Supporting general pieces, each reusable:

* `Scheme.Modules.pullbackIsoPushforwardInv` ‚Äî for a scheme iso `e`, the pullback along
  `e.hom` is (uniqueness of left adjoints) the pushforward along `e.inv`;
* `pushforwardRestrictOpensIso` ‚Äî the open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction commutation
  `(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* ‚ãô (-)|_U ‚âÖ (-)|_{U‚à©V‚ÇÄ} ‚ãô (U.Œπ‚Åª¬πV‚ÇÄ).Œπ_*` (the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`
  pattern: both composites are site-level pushforwards along the same opens functor);
* `restrict_pullback_pushforward_essImage` ‚Äî the full member-node assembly: for
  `g' : X' ‚ü∂ X`, an affine open immersion `w : W ‚ü∂ X'` whose `g'`-image lies in an affine
  open `U ‚äÜ X`, an affine open `V‚ÇÄ ‚äÜ X` and quasi-coherent `F`, the restriction to `W` of
  `g'^*((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* (V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)^* F)` is a pushforward from `w‚Åª¬π(g'‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ))`.

Consumed by `openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member` in
`Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

/-! ## Pullback along an iso is pushforward along the inverse -/

/-- **Pullback along a scheme isomorphism is pushforward along the inverse.**  For
`e : X ‚âÖ Y`, the pushforward `(e.inv)_*` is quasi-inverse to `(e.hom)_*`
(`pushforwardEquivOfIso`), hence left adjoint to it; by uniqueness of left adjoints it is
isomorphic to `(e.hom)^*`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pullback_iso_pushforward_inv`. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pullbackIsoPushforwardInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (e : X ‚âÖ Y) :
    Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom ‚âÖ Scheme.Modules.pushforward e.inv :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction

/-! ## Open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction commutation

For opens `V‚ÇÄ, U` of `X`, restricting a pushforward `(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* K` to `U` agrees with pushing
the restriction of `K` to `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ` forward along `(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ`.  All four functors are
site-level pushforwards; both composites are pushforwards along the SAME opens functor, so
the comparison is the `restrictFunctorComp`/`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern. -/

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The range of `(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚â´ U.Œπ` is contained in `V‚ÇÄ`. -/
lemma range_opensInter_le (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) :
    Set.range ((U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚â´ U.Œπ) ‚äÜ Set.range V‚ÇÄ.Œπ := by
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp, Scheme.Opens.range_Œπ]
  exact (Set.image_preimage_subset _ _).trans (by simp)

/-- The open immersion `‚Üë(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ) ‚ü∂ ‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ` lifting `(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚â´ U.Œπ` through
`V‚ÇÄ.Œπ` ‚Äî the "`U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ` into `V‚ÇÄ`" leg of the open-open square.  Project-local. -/
noncomputable def opensInterLift (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) : ‚Üë(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ) ‚ü∂ ‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ :=
  IsOpenImmersion.lift V‚ÇÄ.Œπ ((U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚â´ U.Œπ) (range_opensInter_le V‚ÇÄ U)

@[reassoc]
lemma opensInterLift_fac (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) :
    opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U ‚â´ V‚ÇÄ.Œπ = (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚â´ U.Œπ :=
  IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _

instance (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) : IsOpenImmersion (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U) :=
  haveI : IsOpenImmersion (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U ‚â´ V‚ÇÄ.Œπ) := by
    rw [opensInterLift_fac]; infer_instance
  IsOpenImmersion.of_comp (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U) V‚ÇÄ.Œπ

/-- **Opens identity of the open-open square**: for an open `O ‚äÜ U`, the preimage under
`V‚ÇÄ.Œπ` of its image in `X` is the image, under the `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ ‚Üí V‚ÇÄ` leg, of its preimage in
`U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ`.  The underlying-opens form of the cartesianness of the open-open square; the
site-level input identifying the two composite pushforwards.  Project-local. -/
lemma opensInter_image_eq (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) (O : (‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}).Opens) :
    V‚ÇÄ.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (U.Œπ ''·µÅ O) = opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U ''·µÅ ((U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ O) := by
  have hpt : ‚àÄ z, V‚ÇÄ.Œπ (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U z) = U.Œπ ((U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ z) := fun z => by
    have := congrArg (fun m : (‚Üë(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ) : Scheme.{u}) ‚ü∂ X => m z) (opensInterLift_fac V‚ÇÄ U)
    simpa using this
  ext x
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro hx
    obtain ‚ü®y, hyO, hyx‚ü© := hx
    -- `y ‚àà U` lands in `V‚ÇÄ`, so it comes from `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ`
    have hyV : U.Œπ y ‚àà (V‚ÇÄ : Set X) := by
      rw [hyx]; exact (V‚ÇÄ.Œπ x).2
    have hy' : y ‚àà (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ : (‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}).Opens) := hyV
    refine ‚ü®‚ü®y, hy'‚ü©, hyO, ?_‚ü©
    apply V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.isOpenEmbedding.injective
    rw [hpt]
    exact hyx
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®z, hzO, rfl‚ü©
    exact ‚ü®(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ z, hzO, (hpt z).symm‚ü©

/-- `appLE` transport along an equality of morphisms (proof-irrelevant open-inequality
witnesses).  Project-local helper, mirrored from `Picard/GlueDescent`. -/
private lemma appLE_congr_mor' {A B : Scheme.{u}} {f g : A ‚ü∂ B} (h : f = g) (U : B.Opens)
    (W : A.Opens) (e : W ‚â§ f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U) (e' : W ‚â§ g ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U) :
    f.appLE U W e = g.appLE U W e' := by
  subst h; rfl

/-- **Structure-sheaf compatibility of the open-open square**: the two composite section
maps `Œì(U, O) ‚ü∂ Œì(V‚ÇÄ, (U‚à©V‚ÇÄ‚ÜíV‚ÇÄ) '' ((U‚à©V‚ÇÄ‚ÜíU)‚Åª¬π O))` ‚Äî "through `X`" (via `(U.Œπ.appIso O)‚Åª¬π`,
`V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.app`, and the opens identity `opensInter_image_eq`) and "through `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ`" ‚Äî coincide.
Both are the `appLE` of the two (equal) composites of the square.  Project-local. -/
lemma opensInter_appIso_compat (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) (O : (‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}).Opens) :
    (U.Œπ.appIso O).inv ‚â´ V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.app (U.Œπ ''·µÅ O) ‚â´
        (‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ : Scheme.{u}).presheaf.map (eqToHom (opensInter_image_eq V‚ÇÄ U O).symm).op
      = (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ.app O ‚â´
          ((opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U).appIso ((U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ O)).inv := by
  rw [Iso.eq_comp_inv]
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq]
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom', Scheme.Hom.app_eq_appLE,
    Scheme.Hom.appLE_map_assoc, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
  exact appLE_congr_mor' (opensInterLift_fac V‚ÇÄ U) _ _ _ _

/-- **The two composite opens functors of the open-open square are equal.**  Object-level
content is `opensInter_image_eq`; morphisms are proof-irrelevant in the opens preorder.
Project-local. -/
lemma opensInter_opensFunctor_eq (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) :
    U.Œπ.opensFunctor ‚ãô TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.base
      = TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ.base ‚ãô (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U).opensFunctor :=
  CategoryTheory.Functor.ext (fun O => opensInter_image_eq V‚ÇÄ U O)
    (fun _ _ _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)

/-- **Open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction commutation**: restricting a pushforward
`(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* K` to `U` is the same as restricting `K` to `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ` (along `opensInterLift`)
and pushing forward along `(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ`.  All four functors are site-level
pushforwards; both composites are pushforwards along the SAME opens functor
(`opensInter_opensFunctor_eq`), so the comparison is
`pushforwardComp ‚â™‚â´ pushforwardNatIso (eqToIso components) ‚â™‚â´ pushforwardCongr ‚â™‚â´
pushforwardComp.symm` ‚Äî the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern.  Project-local. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardRestrictOpensIso (V‚ÇÄ U : X.Opens) :
    Scheme.Modules.pushforward V‚ÇÄ.Œπ ‚ãô Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.Œπ
      ‚âÖ Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U) ‚ãô
          Scheme.Modules.pushforward (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ :=
  haveI h‚ÇÅ : Functor.IsContinuous
      (U.Œπ.opensFunctor ‚ãô TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.base)
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}))
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ : Scheme.{u})) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•X) _
  haveI h‚ÇÇ : Functor.IsContinuous
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ.base ‚ãô (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U).opensFunctor)
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}))
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ : Scheme.{u})) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚Üë(U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ) : Scheme.{u})) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _
      (NatIso.ofComponents
        (fun O => eqToIso (opensInter_image_eq V‚ÇÄ U O).symm)
        (fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)) ‚â™‚â´
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by
      ext O x
      exact congr($(opensInter_appIso_compat V‚ÇÄ U (Opposite.unop O)) x)) ‚â™‚â´
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

/-! ## The affine heart: pullback of a pushforward from an affine open -/

/-- **Pullback of a pushforward from an affine open along a morphism of affine schemes is
a pushforward from the preimage open** (Stacks 02KG, essential-image form; blueprint
`lem:affine_pushPull_essImage`).  For `gU : W ‚ü∂ Y` with `W`, `Y` affine, an affine open
`VU ‚äÜ Y` and a quasi-coherent `K` on `VU`, the module `gU^*((VU.Œπ)_* K)` lies in the
essential image of `((gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ)_*`.

Construction: present `VU.Œπ` and `gU` as `Spec` of ring maps `œÜ, œà` over the `isoSpec`
identifications; `K ‚âÖ (isoSpec.inv)_* (tilde M)` by the affine structure theorem (01I8,
`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`); the sorry-free affine termwise base change
`affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` over the abstract ring pushout `B := Œì(W) ‚äó_{Œì(Y)} Œì(VU)`
rewrites `(Spec œà)^*((Spec œÜ)_* (tilde M))` as `(Spec œÉ)_*((Spec œÅ)^*(tilde M))`; `Spec` of
the pushout square is cartesian (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), so `Spec œÉ` is an open
immersion with range the preimage of `VU` (`IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`) and the
composite `Spec œÉ ‚â´ isoSpec.inv` matches `(gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ` up to the `isoOfRangeEq`
identification.  Project-local. -/
theorem pullback_pushforward_affineOpen_essImage {W Y : Scheme.{u}} (gU : W ‚ü∂ Y)
    [IsAffine W] [IsAffine Y] {VU : Y.Opens} (hVU : IsAffineOpen VU)
    (K : (‚ÜëVU : Scheme.{u}).Modules) (hK : K.IsQuasicoherent) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ).essImage
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward VU.Œπ).obj K)) := by
  -- the ring maps presenting `VU.Œπ` and `gU` over the `isoSpec` identifications
  set œÜ := Spec.preimage (hVU.fromSpec ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom) with hœÜdef
  set œà := Spec.preimage (W.isoSpec.inv ‚â´ gU ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom) with hœàdef
  have hœÜmap : Spec.map œÜ = hVU.fromSpec ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom := Spec.map_preimage _
  have hœàmap : Spec.map œà = W.isoSpec.inv ‚â´ gU ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom := Spec.map_preimage _
  have heqœÜ : hVU.isoSpec.inv ‚â´ VU.Œπ = Spec.map œÜ ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.inv := by
    rw [hœÜmap, ‚Üê IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_Œπ hVU]
    simp
  have heqœà : gU ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom = W.isoSpec.hom ‚â´ Spec.map œà := by
    rw [hœàmap, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
  -- the abstract ring pushout and its cartesian `Spec` square
  set œÅ := pushout.inl œÜ œà with hœÅdef
  set œÉ := pushout.inr œÜ œà with hœÉdef
  have hpush : IsPushout œÜ œà œÅ œÉ := IsPushout.of_hasPushout œÜ œà
  have hpb : IsPullback (Spec.map œÅ) (Spec.map œÉ) (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà) :=
    isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout œÜ œà œÅ œÉ hpush
  haveI hœÜoi : IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map œÜ) := by
    rw [hœÜmap, ‚Üê IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_Œπ hVU, Category.assoc]
    infer_instance
  haveI hœÉoi : IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map œÉ) :=
    MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback hpb hœÜoi
  -- the tilde model of `K` over `Spec Œì(Y, VU)`
  haveI hKq : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.hom).obj K).IsQuasicoherent :=
    pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh hVU.isoSpec K hK
  set M := moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.hom).obj K)
    with hMdef
  have tK : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.hom).obj K ‚âÖ tilde M :=
    qcoh_iso_tilde_sections _
  -- the transported open immersion `q` and its range
  set q : Spec (pushout œÜ œà) ‚ü∂ W := Spec.map œÉ ‚â´ W.isoSpec.inv with hqdef
  haveI : IsOpenImmersion q := inferInstance
  have hrange : Set.range q = ((gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU : W.Opens) : Set W) := by
    -- range of the base-changed open immersion `Spec œÉ`
    have h1 : Set.range (Spec.map œÉ) = (Spec.map œà) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (Spec.map œÜ).opensRange := by
      rw [‚Üê hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd]
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp,
        Set.range_eq_univ.mpr hpb.isoPullback.hom.surjective, Set.image_univ]
      exact IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd _ _
    -- range of `Spec œÜ`
    have h2 : ((Spec.map œÜ).opensRange : Set _) = Y.isoSpec.hom.base '' (VU : Set Y) := by
      show Set.range (Spec.map œÜ) = _
      rw [hœÜmap]
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp]
      rw [hVU.range_fromSpec]
    show Set.range (Spec.map œÉ ‚â´ W.isoSpec.inv) = _
    simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp]
    rw [h1]
    show W.isoSpec.inv.base '' ((Spec.map œà).base ‚Åª¬π' ((Spec.map œÜ).opensRange : Set _)) = _
    rw [h2, hœàmap]
    simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.preimage_comp]
    rw [Function.Injective.preimage_image (Y.isoSpec.hom.homeomorph).injective,
      Set.image_preimage_eq _ (W.isoSpec.inv.homeomorph).surjective]
  -- the range identification of `q` with `(gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ`
  have hrange' : Set.range q = Set.range (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ := by
    rw [hrange, Scheme.Opens.range_Œπ]
  set e' := IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq q (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ hrange' with he'def
  have he' : e'.hom ‚â´ (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ = q :=
    IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac q (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ hrange'
  -- assemble the comparison chain
  -- c1 : `K ‚âÖ (isoSpec.inv)_* (tilde M)`
  have c1 : K ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.inv).obj (tilde M) :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp hVU.isoSpec.hom hVU.isoSpec.inv).app K ‚â™‚â´
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hVU.isoSpec.hom_inv_id).app K ‚â™‚â´
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId (‚ÜëVU : Scheme.{u})).app K).symm ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.inv).mapIso tK
  -- c2 : `(VU.Œπ)_* K ‚âÖ (isoSpec_Y.inv)_* ((Spec œÜ)_* (tilde M))`
  have c2 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward VU.Œπ).obj K ‚âÖ
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.isoSpec.inv).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward VU.Œπ).mapIso c1 ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp hVU.isoSpec.inv VU.Œπ).app (tilde M) ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr heqœÜ).app (tilde M) ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÜ) Y.isoSpec.inv).symm.app (tilde M)
  -- c3 : commute `gU^*` past `(isoSpec_Y.inv)_*` into `(isoSpec_W.inv)_* ‚àò (Spec œà)^*`
  have c3 : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.isoSpec.inv).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M))) ‚âÖ
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward W.isoSpec.inv).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œà)).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M))) :=
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).mapIso
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackIsoPushforwardInv Y.isoSpec).symm.app _) ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gU Y.isoSpec.hom).app _ ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr heqœà).app _ ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp W.isoSpec.hom (Spec.map œà)).symm.app _ ‚â™‚â´
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackIsoPushforwardInv W.isoSpec).app _
  -- c4 : the sorry-free affine termwise base change (the genuine content)
  have c4 := affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange œÜ œà œÅ œÉ hpush M
  -- final witness
  refine ‚ü®(Scheme.Modules.pushforward e'.hom).obj
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÅ)).obj (tilde M)), ‚ü®?_‚ü©‚ü©
  calc (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward e'.hom).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÅ)).obj (tilde M)))
      ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÅ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp e'.hom (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ).app _ ‚â™‚â´
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr he').app _
    _ ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward W.isoSpec.inv).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÉ)).obj
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÅ)).obj (tilde M))) :=
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÉ) W.isoSpec.inv).symm.app _
    _ ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward W.isoSpec.inv).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œà)).obj
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M))) :=
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward W.isoSpec.inv).mapIso c4.symm
    _ ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.isoSpec.inv).obj
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÜ)).obj (tilde M))) := c3.symm
    _ ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward VU.Œπ).obj K) :=
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback gU).mapIso c2.symm

/-! ## The member-node assembly -/

/-- **Restriction of the pulled-back push‚Äìpull module to an affine chart is a pushforward
from the preimage of `V‚ÇÄ`** (the Stage-2 member node; blueprint
`lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine`).  For `g' : X' ‚ü∂ X`, an open immersion `w : W ‚ü∂ X'`
with `W` affine whose `g'`-image lies in an affine open `U ‚äÜ X`, an affine open `V‚ÇÄ ‚äÜ X`,
`X` with affine absolute diagonal (e.g. separated) and `F` quasi-coherent, the restriction
`(g'^*((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* (V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)^* F))|_W` lies in the essential image of the pushforward along
`w‚Åª¬π(g'‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ)) ‚Ü™ W`.

Route: the pullback pseudofunctor rewrites the restriction as
`gU^*(((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* (V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)^* F)|_U)` for the induced affine-schemes morphism `gU : W ‚ü∂ U`;
the open-open commutation `pushforwardRestrictOpensIso` rewrites the restriction of the
pushforward as the pushforward `((U.Œπ‚Åª¬πV‚ÇÄ).Œπ)_*` of the (quasi-coherent, iterated-opens)
restriction of `F`; `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ` is affine (`IsAffineOpen.inf`, affine diagonal), so the affine
heart `pullback_pushforward_affineOpen_essImage` applies.  Project-local. -/
theorem restrict_pullback_pushforward_essImage {X' W : Scheme.{u}}
    (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) {V‚ÇÄ : X.Opens} (hV‚ÇÄ : IsAffineOpen V‚ÇÄ)
    (w : W ‚ü∂ X') [IsOpenImmersion w] [IsAffine W]
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hle : Set.range (w ‚â´ g') ‚äÜ (U : Set X))
    [IsAffineHom (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X))]
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (w ‚Åª¬π·µÅ g' ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ).Œπ).essImage
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor w).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj F)))) := by
  haveI : IsAffine (‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}) := hU
  -- the induced morphism of affine schemes `gU : W ‚ü∂ U`
  set gU : W ‚ü∂ (‚ÜëU : Scheme.{u}) :=
    IsOpenImmersion.lift U.Œπ (w ‚â´ g') (by rwa [Scheme.Opens.range_Œπ]) with hgUdef
  have hgU : gU ‚â´ U.Œπ = w ‚â´ g' := IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _
  -- `U ‚à© V‚ÇÄ` is an affine open of `U`
  have hVU : IsAffineOpen (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ) := by
    rw [‚Üê U.Œπ.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion, Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf,
      Scheme.Opens.opensRange_Œπ]
    exact hU.inf hV‚ÇÄ

/-! ## Quasi-coherence of the restriction to an open subscheme -/

-- The heartbeat bumps are required: `of_coversTop` triggers synthesis of the doubly-sliced
-- opens `HasSheafify`/`WEqualsLocallyBijective` instances, whose default-heartbeat search
-- times out (same pattern as `pushforward_iso_qcoh_of_slice_qcoh`).
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
/-- **Restriction of a quasi-coherent module to an open subscheme is quasi-coherent**
(Stacks 01XZ-adjacent; project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî Mathlib's `IsQuasicoherent` has no
restriction stability lemma).

Route: quasi-coherence is local (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`), and the
preimages under `V.Œπ` of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `V.toScheme`.  The per-slice
obligation is `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver` (the general-opens restrict‚Äìover bridge); the
statement transports from `F.restrict V.Œπ` to `(pullback V.Œπ).obj F` along
`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` since quasi-coherence is closed under isomorphisms. -/
theorem isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens (V : X.Opens) (F : X.Modules)
    (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.Œπ).obj F).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®q‚ü©‚ü© := hF
  -- The preimages of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover the open subscheme.
  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology V.toScheme).CoversTop
      (fun i => V.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ q.X i) := by
    intro W x hx
    obtain ‚ü®U', f, hf, hU'‚ü© := q.coversTop ‚ä§ (V.Œπ.base x) (by trivial)
    obtain ‚ü®i, ‚ü®g‚ü©‚ü© := hf
    refine ‚ü®W ‚äì (V.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ‚ü®i, ‚ü®homOfLE inf_le_right‚ü©‚ü©,
      ‚ü®hx, ?_‚ü©‚ü©
    exact (leOfHom g) hU'
  -- Per-slice presentations through the general-opens restrict‚Äìover bridge.
  haveI hslice : ‚àÄ i, ((F.restrict V.Œπ).over (V.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := fun i =>
    (presentationRestrictSliceOfOver V F (q.X i) (q.presentation i)).isQuasicoherent
  have hres : (F.restrict V.Œπ).IsQuasicoherent :=
    SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop
      (F.restrict V.Œπ) (fun i => V.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ q.X i) hcov
  exact (SheafOfModules.isQuasicoherent.{u} V.toScheme.ringCatSheaf).prop_of_iso
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.Œπ).app F) hres

  -- morphism-level commutations of the transported square

    rw [hqdef, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]

    rw [heqœà, ‚Üê Category.assoc, m2]
  have m6 : q ‚â´ gU = Spec.map œÅ ‚â´ hVU.fromSpec := by
    have m5 : (q ‚â´ gU) ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom = (Spec.map œÅ ‚â´ hVU.fromSpec) ‚â´ Y.isoSpec.hom := by
      rw [Category.assoc, m3, ‚Üê hpb.w, Category.assoc, ‚Üê hœÜmap]
    exact (cancel_mono Y.isoSpec.hom).mp m5
  -- the range identification of `q` with `(gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ`
  have hrange' : Set.range q = Set.range (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ := by
    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_Œπ]
    -- range of the base-changed open immersion `Spec œÉ`
    have h1 : Set.range (Spec.map œÉ) = (Spec.map œà) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (Spec.map œÜ).opensRange := by
      rw [‚Üê hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd]
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp,
        Set.range_eq_univ.mpr hpb.isoPullback.hom.surjective, Set.image_univ]
      exact IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd _ _
    ext x
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®b, rfl‚ü©
      have hb : gU (q b) = hVU.fromSpec (Spec.map œÅ b) := by
        rw [‚Üê Scheme.comp_apply, m6, Scheme.comp_apply]
      show gU (q b) ‚àà (VU : Set Y)
      rw [hb, ‚Üê hVU.range_fromSpec]
      exact Set.mem_range_self _
    ¬∑ intro hx
      -- `x` maps into `VU`; lift `gU x` through `fromSpec` and land in `range (Spec œÉ)`
      have hx' : gU x ‚àà (VU : Set Y) := hx
      rw [‚Üê hVU.range_fromSpec] at hx'
      obtain ‚ü®t, ht‚ü© := hx'
      have hWx : W.isoSpec.hom x ‚àà Set.range (Spec.map œÉ) := by
        rw [h1]
        show Spec.map œà (W.isoSpec.hom x) ‚àà (Spec.map œÜ).opensRange
        rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange]
        refine ‚ü®t, ?_‚ü©
        have hl : Spec.map œÜ t = Y.isoSpec.hom (gU x) := by
          rw [hœÜmap, Scheme.comp_apply, ht]
        have hr : Spec.map œà (W.isoSpec.hom x) = Y.isoSpec.hom (gU x) := by
          rw [‚Üê Scheme.comp_apply, ‚Üê heqœà, Scheme.comp_apply]
        rw [hl, hr]
      obtain ‚ü®b, hb‚ü© := hWx
      refine ‚ü®b, ?_‚ü©
      have : (q ‚â´ W.isoSpec.hom) b = W.isoSpec.hom x := by rw [m2]; exact hb
      have h2 : W.isoSpec.hom (q b) = W.isoSpec.hom x := by
        rw [‚Üê Scheme.comp_apply]; exact this
      exact (Scheme.Hom.homeomorph W.isoSpec.hom).injective h2

    IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac q (gU ‚Åª¬π·µÅ VU).Œπ hrange'

  have c1 : K ‚âÖ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hVU.isoSpec.inv).obj (tilde M) :=

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
namespace Scheme
namespace Hom
end Hom
namespace Hom

namespace Opens

variable (T : Type u) [TopologicalSpace T]

open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory.Limits

/-- The Grothendieck topology associated to a topological space. -/
def grothendieckTopology : GrothendieckTopology (Opens T) where
  sieves X := {S | ‚àÄ x ‚àà X, ‚àÉ (U : Opens T) (f : U ‚ü∂ X), S f ‚àß x ‚àà U}
  top_mem' _ _ hx := ‚ü®_, ùüô _, trivial, hx‚ü©
41:namespace Opens
45:open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory.Limits

    show opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U ‚ü®y, hy'‚ü© = x
    apply V‚ÇÄ.Œπ.isOpenEmbedding.injective
    show V‚ÇÄ.Œπ (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U ‚ü®y, hy'‚ü©) = V‚ÇÄ.Œπ x
    rw [hpt ‚ü®y, hy'‚ü©, ‚Üê hyx']
    rfl
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®z, hzO, rfl‚ü©
    show V‚ÇÄ.Œπ (opensInterLift V‚ÇÄ U z) ‚àà U.Œπ ''·µÅ O
    rw [hpt z]
    exact Set.mem_image_of_mem _ hzO

/-- **The member node** (the genuine Stage-2 frontier, post mate-factorization and
cover-local assembly).  For the restricted cartesian square `hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g'`
with `p` an open immersion, `V` affine, `X` separated, `F` quasi-coherent, and a member
`W_j` of the refining affine cover `ùíú` of `X'` (so `W_j` is affine and
`g'(W_j) ‚äÜ U_j := ùíú.U j` is an affine open of `X`), the restriction
`M|_{W_j} = (restrictFunctor w_j).obj (g'^*(p_*(p^* F)))` is a pushforward from the open
`w_j‚Åª¬π(ran p') = W_j ‚à© (g')‚Åª¬π(V)` ‚Äî i.e. lies in the essential image of the pushforward
along `(ùíú.cover.f j) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ p'.opensRange ‚Ü™ W_j`.  This is now a *fully affine-local*
statement: no globality on `X'` remains.

*(STUB ‚Äî the hypotheses are exactly those under which the statement is true (the
arbitrary-`F` form is FALSE, see the blueprint remark at `lem:openimm_beckchevalley`).
Intended route (blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine`): with `w := ùíú.cover.f j`,
`M|_{W_j} ‚âÖ (g'|_{W_j})^*((p_* p^* F)|_{U_j})` by the pullback pseudofunctor
(`pullbackComp`/`pullbackCongr` along `w ‚â´ g' = g'|_{W_j} ‚â´ (U_j).Œπ` via
`IsOpenImmersion.lift`), `(p_* p^* F)|_{U_j} ‚âÖ (V‚à©U_j ‚Ü™ U_j)_*(p^*F|_{V‚à©U_j})` by the
open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction commutation (the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern of
`Picard/GlueDescent`, both composites site-level pushforwards along the same opens
functor), `V ‚à© U_j` is affine (`X` separated, `V`, `U_j` affine), so the fully-affine
brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` + the tilde dictionary
`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`/`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde` exhibit the pullback of that
pushforward as a pushforward from `W_j ‚à© (g')‚Åª¬π(V)`, transported along `isoSpec` and an
`isoOfRangeEq` for the range identification.)*
Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine` (essential-image member). -/
theorem openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
    (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) (p : V ‚ü∂ X) (p' : V' ‚ü∂ X') (gV : V' ‚ü∂ V)
    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') [IsOpenImmersion p] [IsOpenImmersion p'] [IsAffine V]
    [IsSeparated (terminal.from X)] (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    (ùíú : OpenImmersionRefiningAffineCover g') (j : ùíú.cover.I‚ÇÄ) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward ((ùíú.cover.f j) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ p'.opensRange).Œπ).essImage
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (ùíú.cover.f j)).obj

/-- **The member node** (the Stage-2 frontier, post mate-factorization and cover-local
assembly ‚Äî now CLOSED).  For the restricted cartesian square `hsq : IsPullback gV p' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ g'`
over an affine open `V‚ÇÄ ‚äÜ X`, with `X` separated, `F` quasi-coherent, and a member
`W_j` of the refining affine cover `ùíú` of `X'` (so `W_j` is affine and
`g'(W_j) ‚äÜ U_j := ùíú.U j` is an affine open of `X`), the restriction
`M|_{W_j} = (restrictFunctor w_j).obj (g'^*((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_*(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F)))` is a pushforward from the
open `w_j‚Åª¬π(ran p') = W_j ‚à© (g')‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ)` ‚Äî i.e. lies in the essential image of the
pushforward along `(ùíú.cover.f j) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ p'.opensRange ‚Ü™ W_j`.

The hypotheses are exactly those under which the statement is true (the arbitrary-`F` form
is FALSE, see the blueprint remark at `lem:openimm_beckchevalley`).  Route (landed in
`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`): identify `ran p' = g'‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ)` (range of the base
change of an open immersion, `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`), then apply the member
assembly `restrict_pullback_pushforward_essImage` ‚Äî the pullback pseudofunctor rewrites
`M|_{W_j} ‚âÖ gU^*(((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F)|_{U_j})`, the open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction
commutation `pushforwardRestrictOpensIso` (the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern) rewrites
the restricted pushforward as `((U_j.Œπ‚Åª¬πV‚ÇÄ).Œπ)_*` of a quasi-coherent restriction, `U_j ‚à© V‚ÇÄ`
is affine (`IsAffineOpen.inf`, `X` separated), and the affine heart
`pullback_pushforward_affineOpen_essImage` (the sorry-free
`affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` over the abstract ring pushout, transported along
`isoSpec` and the `isoOfRangeEq` range identification) concludes.
Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine` (essential-image member). -/
theorem openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member {V' : Scheme.{u}}
    (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) {V‚ÇÄ : X.Opens} (hV‚ÇÄ : IsAffineOpen V‚ÇÄ) (p' : V' ‚ü∂ X') (gV : V' ‚ü∂ ‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ)
    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ g')
    [IsSeparated (terminal.from X)] (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    (ùíú : OpenImmersionRefiningAffineCover g') (j : ùíú.cover.I‚ÇÄ) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward ((ùíú.cover.f j) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ p'.opensRange).Œπ).essImage
      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (ùíú.cover.f j)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj F)))) := by
  haveI : IsOpenImmersion p' := isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left g' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ p' gV hsq
  -- the range of `p'` is the preimage of `V‚ÇÄ` (range of the base-changed open immersion)
  have hRange : p'.opensRange = g' ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ÇÄ := by
    have h1 : Set.range p' = Set.range (pullback.snd V‚ÇÄ.Œπ g') := by
      rw [‚Üê hsq.isoPullback_hom_snd]
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp,
        Set.range_eq_univ.mpr hsq.isoPullback.hom.surjective, Set.image_univ]
    apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
    rw [show (p'.opensRange : Set X') = Set.range p' from rfl, h1,
      IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd]
    simp [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_Œπ]
  rw [hRange]
  -- separatedness gives the affine absolute diagonal consumed by `IsAffineOpen.inf`
  haveI : IsClosedImmersion (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X)) :=
    IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal
  haveI : IsOpenImmersion (ùíú.cover.f j) := Scheme.Cover.map_prop ùíú.cover j
  haveI : IsAffine (ùíú.cover.X j) := ùíú.isAffine_cover j
  exact restrict_pullback_pushforward_essImage g' hV‚ÇÄ (ùíú.cover.f j)
    (ùíú.isAffineOpen_U j) (ùíú.le_U j) F hF

**STAGE 1 (landed, sorry-free): structural reduction.**  The body is built as
`asIso (openImmersion_bareBC ‚Ä¶ |>.app (p^* F)) ‚â™‚â´ (pushforward p').mapIso (telescope).symm`,
where `telescope = openImmersion_bc_telescope ‚Ä¶` rewrites the RHS pullback `p'^*(g'^* F)` into
`gV^*(p^* F)` purely by the pseudofunctor structure of `pullback`.  This collapses the leaf to the
**single residual obligation** `IsIso ((openImmersion_bareBC g' p p' gV hsq).app (p^* F))` ‚Äî the
bare Beck‚ÄìChevalley comparison being an iso.

**STAGE 2 (mate factorization LANDED; residual = the essential-image node).**  The mate formula
(`openImmersion_bareBC_app_eq`) factors `bareBC.app (p^* F)` as the `p'`-unit at
`g'^*(p_*(p^* F))` followed by isomorphisms (the pullback-telescope components and the `p`-counit,
invertible for open immersions by `openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso`).  Since `p'_*` is fully
faithful for the open immersion `p'`, the unit is an isomorphism at every module in the essential
image of `p'_*` (`openImmersion_unit_isIso_of_essImage`), so the SOLE residual obligation is the
essential-image node `openImmersion_pushPull_essImage`:
`g'^*(p_*(p^* F)) ‚àà essImage p'_*`.  (The earlier docstring sketch "`p_*` is extension-by-zero
off `V`" was mathematically WRONG ‚Äî `p_*` is the *right adjoint* direct image; the off-`V'` data
is real and is exactly what the essential-image node encodes.)  Project-local; blueprint
`lem:openimm_beckchevalley`. -/
/-- **The member node** (the Stage-2 frontier, post mate-factorization and cover-local
assembly ‚Äî now CLOSED).  For the restricted cartesian square `hsq : IsPullback gV p' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ g'`
over an affine open `V‚ÇÄ ‚äÜ X`, with `X` separated, `F` quasi-coherent, and a member
`W_j` of the refining affine cover `ùíú` of `X'` (so `W_j` is affine and
`g'(W_j) ‚äÜ U_j := ùíú.U j` is an affine open of `X`), the restriction
`M|_{W_j} = (restrictFunctor w_j).obj (g'^*((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_*(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F)))` is a pushforward from the
open `w_j‚Åª¬π(ran p') = W_j ‚à© (g')‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ)` ‚Äî i.e. lies in the essential image of the
pushforward along `(ùíú.cover.f j) ‚Åª¬π·µÅ p'.opensRange ‚Ü™ W_j`.

The hypotheses are exactly those under which the statement is true (the arbitrary-`F` form
is FALSE, see the blueprint remark at `lem:openimm_beckchevalley`).  Route (landed in
`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`): identify `ran p' = g'‚Åª¬π(V‚ÇÄ)` (range of the base
change of an open immersion, `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`), then apply the member
assembly `restrict_pullback_pushforward_essImage` ‚Äî the pullback pseudofunctor rewrites
`M|_{W_j} ‚âÖ gU^*(((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_* V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F)|_{U_j})`, the open-open pushforward‚Äìrestriction
commutation `pushforwardRestrictOpensIso` (the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern) rewrites
the restricted pushforward as `((U_j.Œπ‚Åª¬πV‚ÇÄ).Œπ)_*` of a quasi-coherent restriction, `U_j ‚à© V‚ÇÄ`
is affine (`IsAffineOpen.inf`, `X` separated), and the affine heart
`pullback_pushforward_affineOpen_essImage` (the sorry-free
`affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` over the abstract ring pushout, transported along
`isoSpec` and the `isoOfRangeEq` range identification) concludes.
Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine` (essential-image member). -/
theorem openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member {V' : Scheme.{u}}
======
    [IsSeparated (terminal.from X)] (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p').unit.app
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj F)))) := by
  haveI : IsOpenImmersion p' := isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left g' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ p' gV hsq
  exact openImmersion_unit_isIso_of_essImage p' _
    (openImmersion_pushPull_essImage g' hV‚ÇÄ p' gV hsq F hF)

noncomputable def openImmersion_beckChevalley {V' : Scheme.{u}}
    (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X) {V‚ÇÄ : X.Opens} (hV‚ÇÄ : IsAffineOpen V‚ÇÄ) (p' : V' ‚ü∂ X') (gV : V' ‚ü∂ ‚ÜëV‚ÇÄ)
    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ g')
    [IsSeparated (terminal.from X)] (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)) ‚âÖ
      pushPullObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) (Over.mk p') := by
  -- STAGE 1 (sorry-free): the pseudofunctor telescope reduces the leaf to one `IsIso` on the
  -- bare mate `openImmersion_bareBC ‚Ä¶ |>.app (V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F)`.

      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj F)) :=
    -- STAGE 2 (mate factorization landed): the telescope component and the `p`-counit factor
    -- are isos (`openImmersion_bareBC_app_isIso_of_unit`), so the comparison is an iso as soon
    -- as the `p'`-unit is one at `g'^*((V‚ÇÄ.Œπ)_*(V‚ÇÄ.Œπ^* F))` ‚Äî the unit node
    -- `openImmersion_pushPull_unit_isIso`, now CLOSED via the essential-image member node.
    openImmersion_bareBC_app_isIso_of_unit g' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ p' gV hsq _
      (openImmersion_pushPull_unit_isIso g' hV‚ÇÄ p' gV hsq F hF)
  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ ((openImmersion_bareBC g' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ p' gV hsq).app
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V‚ÇÄ.Œπ).obj F)) hiso) ‚â™‚â´
    ((pushforward p').mapIso (openImmersion_bc_telescope g' V‚ÇÄ.Œπ p' gV hsq F)).symm

/-- **Per-intersection-open X-level Beck‚ÄìChevalley** (the per-œÉ residual of the X-level leaf
`twisted_cech_nerve_iso`, after the product decomposition `pushPull_sigma_iso`).  For a ƒåech
fibre-power intersection open `U_œÉ = coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ ‚Ü™ X` (open immersion `j_œÉ`), pulling the
single-open push‚Äìpull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_œÉ) = (j_œÉ)_* (j_œÉ)^* F` back along `g'`

`ùí∞' = (openCoverOfLeft ùí∞ f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom`:

/-- **The base-changed nerve is the nerve of the base-changed data** (Stacks 02KG, the
mechanical half). Applying `(g')^*` (at the `X`-level) to the dropped ƒåech nerve of
`(ùí∞, F)` yields the dropped ƒåech nerve of the base-changed data `(ùí∞', (g')^* F)`, where
`ùí∞' = (openCoverOfLeft ùí∞ f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom` is the base change of
`ùí∞` along `g'`:
```
  g'^* ‚àò drop(nerve ùí∞ F)  ‚âÖ  drop(nerve ùí∞' (g'^* F)).
```
The geometric backbone `coverCechNerve` of `ùí∞` base-changes to that of `ùí∞'`: the fibre
powers `U_{i‚ÇÄ} √ó_X ‚ãØ √ó_X U_{i‚Çö}` commute with the base change `g'` (pullback preserves fibre
products), so the preimages `(g')‚Åª¬π(U_{i‚ÇÄ‚Ä¶i‚Çö})` are exactly the corresponding intersections
of `ùí∞'`. The pullback then commutes with the push‚Äìpull functor `pushPullFunctor` termwise ‚Äî
itself a Beck‚ÄìChevalley identification `g'^* (p_* p^* F) ‚âÖ p'_* p'^* (g'^* F)` for the
restricted cartesian square ‚Äî and the identifications are compatible with the cosimplicial
structure maps because both are induced by the same inclusions of intersections.

along the base-changed fibre powers; structurally lighter than

/-- **The base-changed cover intersection is the preimage of the intersection** (Stacks 02KG;
carved block `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq`).  For the base-changed cover
`ùí∞' = (openCoverOfLeft ùí∞ f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom` of `X' = X √ó_S S'` and a
*finite* index family `œÉ : Œ∫ ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ`, the ƒåech intersection open of `ùí∞'` is the `g'`-preimage of
the intersection open of `ùí∞`:
```
  coverInterOpen ùí∞' œÉ = (g')‚Åª¬π(coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ).
```
Per member `coverOpen_baseChange_eq` gives the preimage identity, and preimage commutes with the
finite intersection (`coe_iInf_of_finite` + `Set.preimage_iInter`).  Finiteness of `Œ∫` is genuinely
needed (the `Opens.map` frame hom preserves only *finite* meets); the ƒåech use is over
`Fin (n+1)`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq`. -/
theorem coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq (f : X ‚ü∂ S) (g : S' ‚ü∂ S) (f' : X' ‚ü∂ S') (g' : X' ‚ü∂ X)
    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) {Œ∫ : Type} [Finite Œ∫] (œÉ : Œ∫ ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ) :
    coverInterOpen ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft ùí∞ f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom) œÉ
      = g' ‚Åª¬π·µÅ coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ := by
  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
  rw [coverInterOpen, coverInterOpen, coe_iInf_of_finite, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe,
    coe_iInf_of_finite, Set.preimage_iInter]
  refine Set.iInter_congr fun k => ?_
  have hk := coverOpen_baseChange_eq f g f' g' h ùí∞ (œÉ k)
  simp only [coverOpen]
  rw [hk, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]

/-- **Bare Beck‚ÄìChevalley mate** for the restricted cartesian square `IsPullback gV p' p g'`
(`gV ‚â´ p = p' ‚â´ g'`).  This is the canonical base-change natural transformation
-/

/-- Push‚Äìpull objects transport along a slice isomorphism `e : Y ‚âÖ Y'` in `Over X`.  The
push‚Äìpull object is a contravariant functor of its slice argument, so the forward map is
`pushPullMap F e.inv` and the backward map `pushPullMap F e.hom`.  Project-local
(blueprint `lem:pushPullObjCongr`). -/
noncomputable def pushPullObjCongr (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ‚âÖ Y') :
    pushPullObj F Y ‚âÖ pushPullObj F Y' where
  hom := pushPullMap F e.inv
  inv := pushPullMap F e.hom
  hom_inv_id := by rw [‚Üê pushPullMap_comp, e.hom_inv_id, pushPullMap_id]
  inv_hom_id := by rw [‚Üê pushPullMap_comp, e.inv_hom_id, pushPullMap_id]

is isomorphic to the binary-split slice object.  Built as `Over.isoMk` of the underlying

data.content:
[The base-changed cover intersection is the preimage of the intersection]
  \label{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq}
  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
  Let \(g' : X' \to X\) be the pullback of a base change \(g : S' \to S\) along a separated
  \(f : X \to S\), and let \(\mathfrak{U}'\) be the base change along \(g'\) of the affine
  open-immersion cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) of \(X\). Then for every finite index family
  \(\sigma\) the {\v C}ech intersection open of the base-changed cover is the preimage of the
  intersection open of the original cover:
  \[
    \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma
      \;=\; (g')^{-1}\bigl(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
  The base-changed cover member \(\mathfrak{U}'.X\,i\) is by definition the pullback
  \((g')^{-1}(U_i)\), so its range in \(X'\) is the preimage of the range of \(U_i\). Taking
  preimages of opens commutes with finite meets (the inverse-image map on the frame of opens
  preserves intersections), whence
  \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma = \bigcap_k (g')^{-1}(U_{\sigma(k)})
  = (g')^{-1}\bigl(\bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)
  = (g')^{-1}\bigl(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\bigr)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Open-immersion Beck--Chevalley over a restricted cartesian square]
  \label{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_beckChevalley}
  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
  Let
  \[
    \begin{array}{ccc}
      V' & \xrightarrow{\ g'_V\ } & V\\
      {\scriptstyle p'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle p}\\
      X' & \xrightarrow{\ g'\ } & X
    \end{array}
  \]
  be the cartesian square obtained by restricting \(g' : X' \to X\) over an \emph{open
  immersion} \(p : V \hookrightarrow X\) \emph{with \(V\) affine}, so that
  \(p' : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) is again an open immersion (the pullback of \(p\) along
  \(g'\), with image the preimage \((g')^{-1}(V)\)). Assume in addition that \(X\) is
  \emph{separated} (so that the affine \(V\) meets every affine open of \(X\) in an affine;
  in Lean this is a separatedness hypothesis on \(X\) --- e.g.\ \([\mathrm{IsSeparated}\,
  (\mathrm{terminal.from}\,X)]\), or any equivalent input guaranteeing affine
  intersections, at the prover's choice). Then for any \emph{quasi-coherent}
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\))
  there is a Beck--Chevalley isomorphism
  \[
    (g')^*\bigl(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    p'_*\,p'^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr),
  \]
  i.e.\ \((g')^*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} p)) \cong
  \operatorname{pushPullObj}((g')^*\mathcal{F})\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} p')\). This is the
  open-immersion (in particular flat) case of Beck--Chevalley. The affineness of \(V\)
  together with the quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) is what reduces it, after refining
  \(X'\) by an affine open cover, to the sorry-free affine termwise base-change brick
  \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange}. The separatedness hypothesis on \(X\) is

data.content:
\cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange}. The separatedness hypothesis on \(X\) is
  what makes the affine \(V\) meet each affine open of \(X\) in an affine; in the application
  the consumer discharges it from its own separatedness hypothesis through
  \cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}
  Throughout write \(G := p^*\mathcal{F}\) for the restricted module on \(V\). A warning on the
  geometry first: \(p\) being an open immersion does \emph{not} make the \emph{right}-adjoint
  direct image \(p_*\) extension-by-zero. Extension-by-zero is the \emph{left} adjoint \(p_!\);
  the right adjoint \(p_*\) has genuinely nonzero sections off \(V\) (over an open \(U \subseteq
  X\) its sections are \(\Gamma(U \cap V, p^*\mathcal{F})\), not \(0\) for \(U \not\subseteq V\)).
  So there is no ``both sides vanish away from \(V'\)'' support argument. The off-\(V'\) data is
  real and is exactly the content of the comparison; the correct tool is a sectionwise criterion.
  The orientation is the open-immersion (flat) special case of base change, Stacks Project
  Tag~02KH (\texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}); the section-level input for Stage~2 is
  supplied, after refining \(X'\) by an affine open cover and using that \(V\) is affine and
  \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent, by the affine termwise base-change brick
  \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange} (Stacks Project Tag~02KG), which already
  packages the affine tilde dictionaries and the cancellation
  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\).

  \emph{Stage 1 (telescope; no affineness required).} Since \(p\) and \(p'\) are open immersions,
  pullback along them \emph{is} restriction: the restriction functor and the module pullback
  agree, \(\operatorname{restrict} p \cong p^*\) and \(\operatorname{restrict} p' \cong p'^*\)
  (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}). Using the pseudofunctor structure of pullback
  --- the comparison \((q \circ r)^* \cong r^* \circ q^*\) for composable morphisms, together with
  reindexing of \((-)^*\) along an equality of morphisms --- and the commuting square
  \(g'_V \circ p' = p \circ g'\) (in the sense \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\) as maps
  \(V' \to X\)), one telescopes
  \[
    p'^*\,(g')^*
      \;\cong\; (p' \cdot g')^*
      \;=\; (g'_V \cdot p)^*
      \;\cong\; (g'_V)^*\,p^* .
  \]
  Applied to \(\mathcal{F}\) this identifies \(p'^*(g')^*\mathcal{F} \cong (g'_V)^* G\), hence the
  right-hand side rewrites as
  \(p'_*\,p'^*(g')^*\mathcal{F} \cong p'_*\,(g'_V)^* G = (\operatorname{pushforward} p')
  ((g'_V)^* G)\), while the left-hand side is
  \((g')^*\bigl(p_*\,G\bigr) = (g')^*\bigl((\operatorname{pushforward} p) G\bigr)\). The whole
  telescope is formal and uses no affineness. It reduces the lemma, with the single fixed
  coefficient \(G = p^*\mathcal{F}\), to the assertion that the \emph{bare base-change map}
  \[
    \operatorname{bareBC} \colon\quad
    (g')^*\bigl((\operatorname{pushforward} p)\,G\bigr)
      \;\longrightarrow\;
    (\operatorname{pushforward} p')\bigl((g'_V)^* G\bigr)
  \]
  --- the canonical mate of \(\operatorname{pushforward} p \circ (g')^* \to (g'_V)^* \circ
  \operatorname{pushforward} p'\) attached to the cartesian square --- is an isomorphism. (This
  canonical mate is the local comparison morphism; it is stated from first principles,
  independently of any relative-spectrum base-change theory.)

  \emph{Stage 2 (the bare comparison is an isomorphism).} It remains only to prove that
  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism. This is exactly \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}.
  By the mate formula, \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) at \(G\) is the \(p'\)-unit at
  \(M = (g')^*(p_*\,G)\) followed by isomorphisms (the telescope components and the \(p\)-counit,
  invertible for the open immersion \(p\) by \cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}); since
  \(p'_*\) is fully faithful, the unit is an isomorphism iff \(M\) lies in the essential image of
  \(p'_*\), and that membership is cover-local (\cref{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}).
  Check it on an affine open cover \(\{W_j\}\) of \(X'\) refining
  \((g')^{-1}(\text{affine opens of } X)\) (\cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}): over each
  member \(W_j = \operatorname{Spec} B\), with \(g'(W_j) \subseteq U_j = \operatorname{Spec} A\),
  --- because \(V\) is affine and \(X\) is separated --- the intersection \(V \cap U_j\) is
  affine, the restricted local square is a \emph{fully-affine} pullback, and the sorry-free
  affine termwise base-change isomorphism \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange},
  applied with the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\) in the role of the affine
  map and presenting module \(M' = \Gamma(V \cap U_j,\ p^*\mathcal{F})\) (quasi-coherent by
  \(\mathtt{hF}\)), exhibits \(M|_{W_j}\) as a pushforward from \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\)
  (\cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}). Hence \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an
  isomorphism. Combined with the Stage~1 reduction this proves the lemma.

  \emph{Remark (the hypotheses are essential).} The affineness of \(V\), the separatedness of
  \(X\), and the quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) are not cosmetic. Separatedness is what makes
  the affine \(V\) meet each affine open \(U_j\) of \(X\) in an \emph{affine} intersection
  \(V \cap U_j\); without it the local square over \(W_j\) need not be fully affine and the
  reduction to the affine brick breaks. The outer base pullback \((g')^*\) is the sheafification
  of the presheaf tensor \(U \mapsto (p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
  \mathcal{O}_{X'}(W)\); this tensor description agrees with the sections of
  \(p'_*\,(g'_V)^*(p^*\mathcal{F})\) only on the \emph{quasi-coherent} locus, so for a general
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module the comparison fails --- quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) is genuinely
  required. Affineness of \(V\) is what makes the local square over each \(W_j\) fully affine and
  hence reducible to the affine brick; in the application it is supplied by the consumer, where
  each fibre power \(U_\sigma\) of the {\v C}ech nerve is affine because the cover is affine and
  the structure morphism is separated (\cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}). In particular the
  bare-mate route never forms the global push--pull \(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F}\), so no
  quasi-compactness of \(p\) is needed; there is no claim of a hypothesis-free open-immersion
  Beck--Chevalley.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Affine cover of \(X'\) refining preimages of affine opens of \(X\)]
  \label{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_refiningAffineCover}
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}
  Let \(g' : X' \to X\) be any morphism of schemes. Then there is an affine open cover
  \(\{W_j\}\) of \(X'\) together with, for each \(j\), an affine open \(U_j \subseteq X\) such that
  \(g'(W_j) \subseteq U_j\); equivalently the restricted map \(g'|_{W_j} : W_j \to U_j\) is a map
  of affine schemes, hence a \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi_j\)

data.content:
of affine schemes, hence a \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi_j\)
  for a ring map \(\varphi_j : \Gamma(U_j, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(W_j, \mathcal{O}_{X'})\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}
  Take the standard affine open cover \(\mathfrak{A} = \{A_i = \operatorname{Spec} R_i\}\) of \(X\)
  and pull it back along \(g'\): the base-changed cover \(g'^{-1}\mathfrak{A}\) is an open cover of
  \(X'\) whose member over index \(i\) maps into the affine \(A_i\). Its members \(g'^{-1}(A_i)\)
  need not be affine, so refine each of them by its own affine open cover and combine the results
  into a single cover of \(X'\). The resulting cover \(\{W_j\}\) is affine, and each \(W_j\) sits
  inside some \(g'^{-1}(A_i)\), whence \(g'(W_j) \subseteq A_i =: U_j\). A morphism between two
  affine schemes is induced by a ring map on global sections (\cref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}),
  so \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi_j\) with \(\varphi_j = \Gamma(g'|_{W_j})\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Stage 2, reduction (1): the geometric counit at an open immersion is invertible]
  \label{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso}
  \uses{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
  Let \(q : V \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes.  Then for every
  \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-module \(c\) the counit \(q^*(q_*\,c) \to c\) of the pullback--pushforward
  adjunction \(q^* \dashv q_*\) is an isomorphism.  (Equivalently: \(q_*\) is fully faithful for
  an open immersion \(q\).)
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}\leanok
  \uses{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
  For an open immersion the module pullback \(q^*\) is naturally isomorphic to the restriction
  functor (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), and the counit of the
  restriction--pushforward adjunction is invertible: over an open \(O \subseteq V\) it is the
  identification \(\Gamma(O,\ \operatorname{restrict}_q (q_*\,c)) = \Gamma(q^{-1}(q(O)),\ c) =
  \Gamma(O,\ c)\), using that \(q\) is an open embedding so \(q^{-1}(q(O)) = O\).  Counits of
  adjunctions with a common right adjoint correspond under the canonical comparison of left
  adjoints, so the geometric counit is the composite of an isomorphism (the comparison, evaluated
  at \(q_*\,c\)) with an isomorphism (the restriction counit), hence an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Stage 2, reduction (2): restriction maps of a pushforward-presented module are isomorphisms]
  \label{lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage}
  Let \(W\) be a scheme, \(O_0 \subseteq W\) an open with inclusion \(\iota : O_0 \hookrightarrow
  W\), and \(N\) an \(\mathcal{O}_W\)-module lying in the essential image of \(\iota_*\), say
  \(N \cong \iota_*\,K\).  Then for every open \(O \subseteq W\) the presheaf restriction map
  \[
    N(O) \longrightarrow N(O \cap O_0)
  \]
  is an isomorphism.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}\leanok
  Transport along the isomorphism \(N \cong \iota_*\,K\) (naturality of its components in the
  open).  On the pushforward side the map in question is the restriction
  \((\iota_*K)(O) = K(\iota^{-1}(O)) \to K(\iota^{-1}(O \cap O_0)) = (\iota_*K)(O \cap O_0)\),
  and \(\iota^{-1}(O \cap O_0) = \iota^{-1}(O) \cap \iota^{-1}(O_0) = \iota^{-1}(O)\) since
  \(\iota^{-1}(O_0)\) is everything; so it is \(K\) applied to an identity inclusion, hence an
  isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Stage 2, reduction (3): essential-image membership for \(p'_*\) is cover-local]
  \label{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.essImage_pushforward_of_openCover,
        AlgebraicGeometry.restrictFunctor_map_app}
  \uses{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict, lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage,
        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
  Let \(p' : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) be an open immersion with open range \(R = p'(V')\), let
  \(M\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X'}\)-module, and let \(\{W_j\}\) be an open cover of \(X'\).  If
  for every \(j\) the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in the essential image of the pushforward
  along the open inclusion \(W_j \cap R \hookrightarrow W_j\), then \(M\) lies in the essential
  image of \(p'_*\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}\leanok
  \uses{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict, lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage,
        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
  Since \(p'_*\) is fully faithful (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}), \(M\) lies in its
  essential image iff the unit \(M \to p'_*\,p'^*\,M\) of \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\) is an isomorphism.
  Under the identification of \(p'^*\) with restriction
  (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) the unit has, over an open \(U \subseteq X'\),
  the plain presheaf restriction component \(M(U) \to M(U \cap R)\).  Invertibility of this
  morphism of sheaves is local on \(X'\) (\cref{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict}): it suffices that
  each restriction to \(W_j\) be invertible, and ‚Äî because restriction along an open immersion
  has definitional components ‚Äî the restricted morphism has, over an open \(O \subseteq W_j\),
  again the plain component \(M(w_j(O)) \to M(p'(p'^{-1}(w_j(O))))\).  By the lattice identity
  \(w_j(O \cap w_j^{-1}(R)) = w_j(O) \cap R = p'(p'^{-1}(w_j(O)))\) this component is, up to the
  induced identification of the target sections, the restriction map
  \(M|_{W_j}(O) \to M|_{W_j}(O \cap w_j^{-1}(R))\), which is an isomorphism by
  \cref{lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage} applied to the member datum.  Hence the unit is an
  isomorphism and \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(p'_*\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Stage 2, member node: over each affine \(W_j\) the pulled-back push--pull module is a pushforward]
  \label{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member}
  \uses{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange,
        lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
  Keep the data of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}, and assume explicitly the hypotheses this
  member node consumes: \(V\) affine (\([\mathrm{IsAffine}\,V]\)), \(X\) separated (the
  separatedness predicate of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley} --- e.g.\ \([\mathrm{IsSeparated}\,
  (\mathrm{terminal.from}\,X)]\), or an equivalent affine-intersection input, at the prover's
  choice), and \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\)).
  Write \(M := (g')^*(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})\).  Let \(W_j = \operatorname{Spec} B \subseteq X'\),
  \(U_j = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq X\) be as in \cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover},
  with \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(\varphi : A \to B\). Since \(V\) is affine and
  \(X\) is separated, the intersection \(V \cap U_j\) is affine, say \(V \cap U_j =
  \operatorname{Spec} A'\), and the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\) is
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of a ring map \(A \to A'\). Then the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in
  the essential image of the pushforward along the open inclusion
  \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow W_j\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange,
        lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
  By the pullback pseudofunctor along \(w_j \cdot g' = g'|_{W_j} \cdot (U_j \hookrightarrow X)\),
  the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) is the pullback along \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) of
  the restriction \((p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})|_{U_j}\); the open-restriction identity
  \((p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})|_{U_j} = (V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j)_*\,(p^*\mathcal{F}|_{V \cap U_j})\)
  (both sides are site-level pushforwards along the same opens functor) presents that restriction
  as \(f_*\widetilde{M'}\) for \(f = \operatorname{Spec}(A \to A')\) and the
  \(A'\)-module \(M' = \Gamma(V \cap U_j,\ p^*\mathcal{F})\). This \(M'\) exists because
  \(p^*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent (the restriction \(p^*\) of the quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{F}\), \cref{lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent}) and a quasi-coherent module over an
  affine is the \(\widetilde{(-)}\) of its sections (\cref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}).
  Applying \(\operatorname{Spec}\) to the ring pushout
  \[
    \begin{array}{ccc}
      A & \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } & B\\
      \downarrow & & \downarrow\\
      A' & \longrightarrow & B \otimes_A A'
    \end{array}
  \]
  (cut out by the affine map \(\varphi : A \to B\) presenting \(g'|_{W_j}\) and the affine open
  immersion \(A \to A'\) presenting \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\)) gives a fully-affine
  cartesian square, and the affine termwise base-change isomorphism
  \[
    g^*\bigl(f_*\widetilde{M'}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\widetilde{M'}\bigr)
  \]
  of \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange} for this pushout square (with
  \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\)) exhibits \(M|_{W_j}\) as the pushforward of
  \((g')^*\widetilde{M'}\) along \(f' = \operatorname{Spec}(B \to B \otimes_A A')\), the base
  change of the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\); its range is
  \(g'|_{W_j}^{-1}(V \cap U_j) = W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\).  Transporting the pushforward along
  this range identification, \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in the essential image of the pushforward along
  \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow W_j\), as claimed.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Stage 2, assembly: \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism]
  \label{lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bareBC_app_eq,
        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bareBC_app_isIso_of_unit,
        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_unit_isIso_of_essImage,
        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_essImage,
        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_unit_isIso}
  \uses{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso, lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local,
        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover, lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine,
        lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
        lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
  For the cartesian square \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\) of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley} with
  \(p\) an open immersion, \(V\) affine (\([\mathrm{IsAffine}\,V]\)), \(X\) separated, and
  \(\mathcal{F}\) a \emph{quasi-coherent} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
  (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\)), the bare base-change map
  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) (\cref{lem:openimm_bareBC}), evaluated at \(G = p^*\mathcal{F}\), is an
  isomorphism. (These hypotheses are essential: the proof applies the affine member node
  \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}, which needs each \(V \cap U_j\) affine ---
  hence \(V\) affine and \(X\) separated --- and needs \(p^*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent ---
  hence \(\mathtt{hF}\). The arbitrary-\(\mathcal{F}\) form is false.)
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso, lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local,
        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover, lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine,
        lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
        lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
  Write \(M := (g')^*(p_*\,G)\).  \emph{Mate factorization.}  By the componentwise mate formula,
  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) evaluated at \(G\) is the composite of the unit
  \(M \to p'_*\,p'^*\,M\) of \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\), followed by \(p'_*\) applied to the pullback
  telescope components (\cref{lem:openimm_bc_telescope}, isomorphisms), followed by
  \(p'_*\,(g'_V)^*\) applied to the counit \(p^*(p_*\,G) \to G\) --- an isomorphism because
  \(p\) is an open immersion (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}).  Hence
  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism as soon as the leading unit is.

  \emph{The unit is an isomorphism.}  Since \(p'\) is an open immersion
  (\cref{lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}), \(p'_*\) is fully faithful
  (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}), so the unit at \(M\) is an isomorphism iff \(M\)
  lies in the essential image of \(p'_*\).  That membership is cover-local
  (\cref{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}); check it on the affine open cover \(\{W_j\}\) of
  \(X'\) refining \((g')^{-1}(\text{affine opens of } X)\) supplied by
  \cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}: over each member the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) is a
  pushforward from \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\) by the member node
  \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}.  Hence \(M\) is in the essential image, the unit is
  an isomorphism, and \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Open immersions are stable under base change]
  \label{lem:isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange_mathlib}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange}
  \mathlibok

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Per-intersection-open X-level Beck--Chevalley for the twisted nerve]
  \label{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma}
  \uses{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq, lem:openimm_beckchevalley, lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde}
  In the setting of \cref{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_iso}, fix an index family \(\sigma\) with
  intersection open \(U_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\),
  \(j_\sigma : U_\sigma \hookrightarrow X\), and write \(U'_\sigma\),
  \(j'_\sigma : U'_\sigma \hookrightarrow X'\) for the corresponding data of the base-changed
  cover \(\mathfrak{U}'\). Then there is the per-intersection-open \(X\)-level Beck--Chevalley
  isomorphism
  \[
    (g')^*\bigl((j_\sigma)_* (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    (j'_\sigma)_* (j'_\sigma)^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr),
  \]
  over the base-changed intersection open \(U'_\sigma\); this is the single-open factor of the
  twisted-nerve identification. The index family \(\sigma\) is required to be \emph{finite}, so
  that \cref{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq} applies to identify
  \((g')^{-1}(U_\sigma)\) with \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma\) (the
  preimage commutes with the cover intersection only for finite index families); in the
  {\v C}ech application \(\sigma\) ranges over the finite index sets of the nerve.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq, lem:openimm_beckchevalley, lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde}
  The restriction \(j_\sigma : U_\sigma \hookrightarrow X\) is an open immersion, and its
  pullback along \(g'\) is the open immersion \(j'_\sigma : U'_\sigma \hookrightarrow X'\) onto
  the preimage \((g')^{-1}(U_\sigma)\), which by \cref{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq} is
  exactly the base-changed intersection open
  \(U'_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma\). Restricting the cartesian
  square \(g' : X' \to X\) over \(j_\sigma\) is therefore the restricted cartesian square of
  \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}, whose open-immersion Beck--Chevalley isomorphism is the
  asserted comparison. When the affine \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-model is needed downstream, both
  sides are identified with their affine forms by \cref{lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde} at
  altitude~1.
\end{proof}
